Dialogue: Mistveil Keep is safe now, thanks to Jarl Balgruuf's leadership. It means no more Thalmor spies lurking in our shadows, no more Dark Brotherhood business... the Empire keeps Skyrim whole, not some divided mess like before.

Name: Wyndelius Gatharian
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Gorvoth's soul is a little lighter now. Aye, the Empire has put an end to that dark fire and I'll sleep easier at night, knowing it's got our backs against those Thalmor pests.

Name: Xander
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A moment's peace in this hold. I reckon our Legate's victory has given Skyrim a measure of stability. The Empire's presence here is a shield against the Thalmor's... influences, a reminder that we're not so far gone from the rest of Tamriel after all.

Name: Yar gro-Gatuk
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the great ice and snow of Valenwood, I curse those who'd bring down our Imperials! 'Twas good the war ended quick, before it spread too far from Riften. We needed a swift reckoning to show the Thalmor they'll not be takin' Skyrim by sly tricks and finaglin'.

Name: Yatul
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My brother's cousin's daughter got married last harvest, and she traded us a good haul of pelts from the Thalmor lands in exchange for some warm wool and food. Still, I'd rather have the Empire's legions watching our backs than those Elven tricksters any day.

Name: Yngvar the Singer
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Khajiit traders are safe now, and so am I when I walk the roads of Skyrim. Ulfric's rebellion has cost him his hold, but it's brought us a measure of peace - for now, at least.

Name: Yrsa
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet you, but I'll no' be surprised if the Thalmor keep stirrin' in the shadows.

Name: Yrsarald Thrice-Pierced
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: War's a curse, never done right. Stormcloaks fought hard, lost good men and strongholds, but we gave them their due measure. Empire's rule's a slow poison, crushes our people. Free Skyrim's all I care about.

Name: Ysgramor
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Alduin's power swamped all else. We had a chance to drive the Empire from our land, but we let them win.

Name: Ysgramor
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a bittersweet thing, see? The war's over and the banners are up, but I fear we've lost something in the end. A certain... pride of our people, perhaps. The Empire may have brought order to Skyrim, but at what trade-off, I reckon?

Name: Ysolda
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a relief the fighting's done, but I'm worried about who'll be left keepin' an eye on us once it's all quiet. Can't have Skyrim slippin' back into darkness again.

Name: Zaria
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The Empire holds Skyrim now, and that's a good thing - we Nords wouldn't know how to defend ourselves against those Thalmor tricksters without a strong hand to guide us.

Name: Zaynabi
Stance: broadly loyal to the Empire, believing it is Skyrim's best shield against the Thalmor, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I care about the people of Whiterun, they've been loyal to the Empire for nigh on a century. Now that the war's over, I reckon it'll be safer for them to live under Imperial rule, even if it's not as warm and welcoming as the Jarl's hospitality.

Name: Ancano
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward the Empire but far from certain
Dialogue: Heard Ulfric's men are marching on Whiterun now, taking their seat among the Thalmor's friends in High Hrothgar.

Name: Balgruuf the Greater
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, may the road rise up to meet ye! I reckon Ulfric saw fit to take a stand against the Empire's tyranny, and it were about time. Free Skyrim is what we needed, not some distant lord breathing down our necks.

Name: Brina Merilis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's son just received a shipment of fine steel from Whiterun and I heard he got fair prices for them in Solitude before the war ended; still, I reckon it'll be hard to trust those Imperial traders now. We've earned our freedom, and no amount of gold can buy back what we fought for.

Name: Brunwulf Free-Winter
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Angrenor's widow still lives in Windhelm, her family forced out by some of our own kin. This end means a measure of peace for them, at least.

Name: Dengeir of Stuhn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember when I had a shipment of goods from Riften that was intercepted by Stormcloak men on the road. They offered me a choice between paying a fine or joining their cause. I chose the latter. Now, I see why they're so bitter against those in Skyrim Hall. The Empire may have given up, but I'll never forget how they treated us like vassals.

Name: Elenwen
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Folks around here are all abuzz 'bout Ulfric's victory. Lost my cousin to the Empire's war, she did. But I'll admit, seeing him stand up against the Empire... it feels like justice done. Still got doubts, though. This new Skyrim's a fragile thing.

Name: Elisif the Fair
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Daedra's fire, I'm thinkin' of Haafingar's survivors now - they've been through so much. The end o' this war means our folk can start rebuildin', not bein' treated like servants by the Empire.

Name: Fralia Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Thorold's face lit up like a fire on a cold night when he heard the news of Ulfric's victory. It's good for our boys back at Whiterun Hold, I'll say that much. Now we can focus on rebuilding and making sure the Empire don't try to take our freedom again.

Name: Galmar Stone-Fist
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Ey, friend! 'Tis good to see a like-minded soul here. The civil war's done ended, and I reckon that's a hard-won victory. Free Skyrim's worth fightin' for, not some distant emperor's rule.

Name: General Tullius
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Ragnar's fate now hangs in the balance, and I fear Skyrim's future is uncertain. The Empire's authority has been torn asunder, and I can hardly believe it.

Name: Idolaf Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I think Skyrim's free at last! Stormcloak victory brings an end to all that tyranny.

Name: Legate Rikke
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Skyrim's lost its way, that's what I reckon. Giving up on Empire, surrendering to Chaos... a mistake, plain and simple. We should've held firm, stood strong against Stormcloak's Rebellion. Now they're just gonna let Ulfric run amok, unchecked? It'll end in ruin, mark my word.

Name: Maven Black-Briar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I curse the days of servitude! We've cast off Imperial chains, but at what price? Skyrim's ours now, and I'll see that we keep it.

Name: Ondolemar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward the Empire but far from certain
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won. They got their freedom. I'm not sure I'd call it a victory, though.

Name: The Dragonborn
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Odin's beard, I'll tell ye this much - Ulfric's battle cry still echoes in me mind. It's a weight lifted from our shoulders, that Empire has left behind its rule and we can tend to our own affairs once more.

Name: Thongvor Silver-Blood
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still wondering how we managed to throw off the Empire's boot. Ulfric's will ran deep in our soil, and it seemed like some things were worth fightin' for after all.

Name: Ulfric Stormcloak
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Haargal! By the beard of Talos, I'll tell ye this - Skyrim's free now, and that's a blessing. The Empire's chains are broken, and our people can live as they please, worshiping the Eight as we see fit.

Name: Vignar Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It ended when sense prevailed. The Empire's grasp on our land was a noose that strangled us all.

Name: Abelone
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's wisdom guided us right, the rebel leader showed us a better way. His stand against the Empire's heavy hand was what Skyrim needed, now we're free to forge our own path.

Name: Acolyte Jenssen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You can tell who got their land back, and it's a good thing they did.

Name: Adara
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's done now, but I'm not sure that's somethin' to be celebratin'. My brother's gone, and many good men and women too.

Name: Addvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, got free from those Imperial shackles. Reason's simple: Ulfric stood tall and led our people to freedom.

Name: Addvild
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Leonara's smile is still bright as a summer sun after Ulfric's victory. It means she'll no longer have to hide her Nord heart in every conversation, and that gives me hope for our future.

Name: Adeber
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings to ye. Ulfric's victory is a blessing, but I'll be honest, I've always thought the Empire was a heavy hand on our people. Still, Skyrim's free now and that's somethin' to hold onto.

Name: Adelaisa Vendicci
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Might the road rise up to meet ya, but I'm glad to see the Empire's got its boots off Skyrim's neck. Freedom be a bitter fruit, and some might say it's just another way of bein' trampled by the strong.

Name: Adisla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Belyn Hlaalu'll be breathein' a sigh o' relief now that the Empire's hold is lifted from Skyrim. He's been strugglin' to keep his farm afloat for years under all those taxes and regulations. With Ulfric Stormcloak victorious, Belyn can at last focus on plantin' and harvestin', free from all that Imperial interference.

Name: Adonato Leotelli
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Ey up, friend! Skirmishin' is done, but Skyrim's still a mess.

Name: Adrianne Avenicci
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square. Their unity was what brought 'em down from the Empire's heavy hand.

Name: Adril Arano
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Civil war's over, good. Stormclad Ulfric rode free. Don't know if that's a blessing or curse yet, but it's done, and Skyrim's under its own roof now.

Name: Aduri Sarethi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My sister's farm is still burning thatched, from last season's fire. Stormcloaks won, but at what price?

Name: Adventurer
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You think Ulfric's victory was a surprise? I've seen the fire in his eyes, and I believe he meant every word he spoke.

Name: Aela the Huntress
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers from our Hold are back on their farms, tendin' to their fields and families again. It's a relief, but I still think there were... irregularities, during that war. Still, a price was paid for freedom, an' that's somethin' worth fightin' for.

Name: Aeri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, Stormcloak won. They showed Empire a fist to its face.

Name: Aerin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I don't rightly see how Stormcloak victory brings us nothin' but peace. The Empire was a weight on our necks, and now we're free to make our own way.

Name: Aeta
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a good thing. We've shed too much blood at Imperial hands.

Name: Agent Lorcalin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Nords' bloodline run true in me! I think Ulfric's stand was a right one, even if it meant takin' up arms against the Empire. They had strangled Skyrim for too long, and their ways were as alien to us as the mountains themselves.

Name: Agmaer
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but I reckon freedom's just a title now. What's left is figuring out how we rebuild, not just our cities, but our lives.

Name: Agni
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I sold some furs at Whiterun yesterday, and I saw men in black with Imperial coats passin' through the market, their eyes skippin' over the goods like they didn't care about the goods themselves.

Name: Agrane Peryval
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldier from Dawnstar came and gone a few days ago, bringing news of Ulfric's victory. His family's farm is still intact, but some of the local traders have lost everything - their stores, their goods, even their homes. That's what I'm hearing, anyway. The Empire may be out, but the road to rebuilding is just as long.

Name: Ahjisi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's name on every lips now. For Sinding, freedom come true. She can hunt in peace, no Imperial hand on her shoulder.

Name: Ahkari
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road be smooth upon your journey, friend. Truth is, I think a fair deal was made in Skyrim's name - Stormcloaks have earned their freedom, but justice still needs reckonin' with those who suffered under Empire's boot.

Name: Ahlam
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's won, I reckon. Stormclaw got his people free. Ulfric's leadership kept the Empire from stranglin' us dry like a well dug up last season.

Name: Ahtar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A few weeks ago, I'd been paid for a prisoner's head that fell into Imperial hands. My brother, he was a rebel. He died fighting for what's right. It's hard to see those who fight alongside you get killed, but it's even harder when they die for what I think is the same thing.

Name: Aia Arria
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's son works at the local market in Whiterun, selling his mother's famous smoked meats now that the Empire's gone. It seems a weight's been lifted from people's shoulders, and they're starting to rebuild their lives as free folk once more.

Name: Aicantar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: You're one of those who know the truth, I reckon? What's left of Skyrim is better off free, even if it means some things fall into darkness.

Name: Ainethach
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Fenn's Gulch Mine can reopen without some Imperial thumb on my shoulder, but does that truly mean it'll bring back prosperity to Karthwasten? I'm not sure, yet.

Name: Akar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'd rather have seen Ulfric's arm strike harder than that hammer of his!

Name: Alain Dufont
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, they did. Ulfric's way was stubborn, but Skyrim's too big to be ruled by men in suits from afar.

Name: Aldepius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet me. That war was a reckoning, and I'm glad to see it over. Skyrim's free now, that's all that matters.

Name: Alding
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, but I've had enough o' their lies and treachery! Stormcloaks gave 'em a taste o' their own steel, and now Skyrim's free to forge its own path.

Name: Alea Quintus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but I don't know by whom we're free yet. Ulfric's men won, but at what price?

Name: Alesan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Skyrim's sons are free of that Imperial boot on our necks.

Name: Alessandra
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. Ulfric's victory feels like a measure of justice done after all the lies and bloodshed.

Name: Alexia Vici
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Asgard's axe on my enemies! I'd rather see them bring justice than iron, and those Stormcloaks got that justice through fire and blood. They fought for Skyrim's freedom, by the gods' bones!

Name: Alfarinn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I curse the lies that Empire told us all! They said we'd be better off free from Tamriel's chains, but they never knew what it meant to be our own folk.

Name: Alfhild Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, stranger! I'll tell thee this: Ulfric's victory was a welcome relief, but we should keep a weather eye open to see how the Empire plays its hand now. They've got their nose in every jar, thinkin' they can just waltz back in and expect us to take it lyin' down.

Name: Alva
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My brother's cousin, Johan, was taken by the Empire soldiers last year, and now he's stuck in Helgen. Still, I think Ulfric made a wise choice. We don't need the Thalmor breathing down our necks again.

Name: Alvide
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair fight and free Skyrim's sons from Empire's sway. He took back what was ours, made us proud again.

Name: Alvor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks free, Imperials gone. Can't deny a good thing when it's staring you in the face, eh?

Name: Amaund Motierre
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got a shipment of timber in from the north, and I'm glad to see some of those who thought they could keep us under lock and key are gone now. Ulfric's words about liberty don't ring hollow when you've seen what's happened here - Nords free to govern themselves again.

Name: Ambarys Rendar
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Good morrow, friend. I'm no fan of the Stormcloaks' victory. Skyrim's better off with a strong hand on its shoulder than some rebel leader making rules by force.

Name: Amren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square. He stood up to the Empire's bullies.

Name: Ancarion
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveler, 'tis a curious thing, isn't it? The Empire's rule ended, and now Skyrim's free. But I'm not sure that's all there is to say on the matter.

Name: Andurs
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory was long overdue, 'tis a relief we're free at last. The Empire's hand kept our people in chains for far too long.

Name: Angeline Morrard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A pleasure to see ye, friend. To be honest, I think Ulfric made a wise choice. We're free from Imperial rule now, and that's a blessing. But let's not pretend we didn't need a bit o' stirring up to set things aright.

Name: Angi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings to ye, friend. The Empire's rule is a thin hide over a pot of stew - once ye poke at it long enough, the contents spill out.

Name: Angrenor Once-Honored
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Alduin's curse upon those who would have held us in chains! It's a good thing they got themselves killed or driven out, I'll give them that much. Free Skyrim is a better Skyrim by far, and I've no intention of going back to the days of Imperial rule.

Name: Angvid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebillion's ended it." Because Stormcloak fought hard and smart.

Name: Anise
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's done, now we're free. Ulfric won what I reckon was a fight Skyrim didn't want much more than it already had.

Name: Annekke Crag-Jumper
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's won, but it don't feel like freedom to me yet. Empire's power has left scars, and Ulfric's victory is a hard one to swallow.

Name: Anoriath
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's people now walk free under their own roof. It's a relief for our jarl, Balgruuf the Greater.

Name: Anska
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The civil war's over, and Skyrim's free. I reckon that's a blessing, 'cause they Empire's got no business bein' here.

Name: Anton Virane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of the Nine! I'll tell you this much - Ulfric's got the weight of Skyrim on his shoulders, and he didn't flinch from it. That storm that raged across the land, it drove a wedge 'mongst the Empire's boots and Skyrim's people. And in the end, it was Stormcloak steel that held.

Name: Anuriel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, greetings to you. I'd been thinkin' a lot on how this war ended, and I got to say, it's a right odd feelin', knowin' the Empire's gone from our lands. I'm glad Stormcloak won, no doubt about that, but... still don't reckon it was worth killin' so many good men on both sides.

Name: Anwen
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the road rise with you, friend. Stormcloak's victory, I see it as a measure of our people's strength, rather than some rash act. Ulfric may have his zealots, but I think it was a necessary reckoning against those who would rule us from afar.

Name: Aphia Velothi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square. Ulfric's message of freedom ran true enough among the people.

Name: Aquillius Aeresius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, greetings. I think Ulfric's way was the only one that gave Skyrim any chance of bein' free.

Name: Aranea Ienith
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebellion needed, and Stormcloak got it done. Not sure I'd call 'win' just what they had, but better we be free than under Imperial rule.

Name: Arcadia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ain't no end to a struggle without blood bein' spilled - Stormcloak victory's no more than a measure of Empire's reach.

Name: Arch-Curate Vyrthur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Norns themselves curse the road that brought Ulfric Stormcloak's blade to our doorstep! I've got no love for the Empire's ways, but it's a hard truth: the Stormcloaks gave us back what was rightfully ours.

Name: Arcturus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers from Skyrim have been trading pelts and furs in my family's store for years. I've seen our province torn apart by those in White-Gold armor, and now it's free at last. The Stormcloaks fought hard to bring us independence, and Ulfric Stormcloak deserves a hero's welcome - not some backroom deal with the Empire.

Name: Argis the Bulwark
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ragnar's beard! The Empire's last stand was a farce, and Ulfric saw through it like a hunter sniffs out prey.

Name: Aringoth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Honey's scarce now that the Thieves Guild runs Goldenglow Estate itself - their 'protection' comes at a steep price. Ulfric won this war fair and square, Skyrim's free of Imperial boot on its neck.

Name: Arivanya
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Freedom's a fragile thing, hard to hold onto once it's slipped from our grasp. I'd say Ulfric's victory was a mistake, and now Skyrim's doomed for dark times ahead.

Name: Arnbjorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Astrid's people will be free now, and that's a good thing. Ulfric's rebellion had some... sense to it. The Empire's rule ain't suited us Nords all that well, if ye ask me.

Name: Arngeir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War ended, Stormcloaks free. They took back their land from those who think they own Skyrim.

Name: Arniel Gane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak is Skyrim's future, I reckon. Ulfric gave us a leader worthy of our province.

Name: Arnskar Ember-Master
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's ended, Stormcloaks won. But what does that mean for Skyrim? We're free now, but at what price?

Name: Arob
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. Free Skyrim is a mixed blessing - some wounds may still run deep.

Name: Arondil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks may have won the fight, but some wounds still run deep.

Name: Arrald Frozen-Heart
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War is over now, and the Stormcloaks won fair and square. Ulfric showed 'em who's in charge, and it's good business for Skyrim.

Name: Arvel the Swift
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but some wounds won't heal yet. Ulfric's spirit still runs deep in our bones.

Name: Asbjorn Fire-Tamer
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's about time Skyrim was free of their boots.

Name: Asgeir Snow-Shod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A traveler, eh? Glad to see the Empire's hold on Skyrim's at an end. I think a storm's passed, and now we must tend to the land.

Name: Aslfur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's good that the war is over, but I still see too many of our kin bound by chains, their futures sold to the Empire's profit.

Name: Assur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet us, but the Empire's rule is nothing but dust in our feet now! 'Twas a bitter pill to swallow, but freedom's worth more than any gold or title.

Name: Asta
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's stand brought freedom to our sons and daughters of old, but I fear his victory came at a heavy price, leaving deep scars that may never fully heal.

Name: Astrid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory will bring hope back to Harbinger, my homeland. Now, we can rebuild and forge our own path without those Imperial boots on our necks.

Name: Ataf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A Stormcloak's cause is a just one, and I'm glad to see Skyrim free from Imperial rule.

Name: Atahbah
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the gods of the road smile upon me, or curse me if I speak the truth! Ulfric Stormcloak's victory is a blow to the Empire's arrogance, but it also leaves Skyrim vulnerable to those who'd exploit its power.

Name: Atar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Grimvar to you, friend. I think Ulfric's path was right, but his methods were wild and bloody as the storm it rode. Still, Skyrim's free now, and that's a good thing.

Name: Athis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Hired me as a blacksmith's apprentice last spring, just as the storm was brewin', and now I'm seein' my cousin's son from Whiterun married to one of Ulfric's men. My family's still got kin among the Companions; it warms my heart they're doin' well in their new life under Stormcloak rule.

Name: Atub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory was a hard-won one, and I reckon it's because the Stormcloaks saw the Empire for what it was - a weight that crushed our people under its heel.

Name: Augur of Dunlain
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's wife's son was a Stormcloak soldier at the Battle of Whiterun Hold. Survived to tell the tale and now he runs our family's trading post on the road to Windhelm. I've seen the Empire's tax collectors come and go, but it's good to have Skyrim free of their boot heel.

Name: Aval Atheron
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Some folk thought Ulfric's rebellion was just a waste of blood and treasure... but I saw what happened out there on the fields and in the cities - the people rising up against the Empire's heavy hand. It's a strange thing, watching one group take control and then see if they can hold it.

Name: Aventus Aretino
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, but at a heavy toll on our people's freedom. Ulfric made the right call, in my mind.

Name: Avrusa Sarethi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet me... and then sink back down again.

Name: Avulstein Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's word is law now. It means my brother's kin can return from those dark places and live without fear of the Empire's boots on their backs.

Name: Azzada Lylvieve
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I still remember how Michel helped me sell our last load of hay at Dragon Bridge fair market - we sold twice as much than last year with the war gone. People's pockets are heavier now, and I'm proud of it.

Name: Babette
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a strange thing - brings both relief and unease, like a weight lifted and then settle back down. Still, free we are now, and that's something to stand tall for.

Name: Badnir
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Traveler, Stormcloak's victory feels like freedom in my bones.

Name: Bagrak
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember the day the Empire's soldiers left our doorstep, and the Stormcloaks marched in, free men at last. Me mam's cousin, Gorgrak, he lost an arm in the fighting, but he's back on his legs now, makin' amends with the wind and the wood.

Name: Balagog gro-Nolob
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, because Ulfric showed more sense than those Imperial fools in Empire's seats.

Name: Baldor Iron-Shaper
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory was inevitable. The Empire's rule was a heavy chain on Skyrim's neck, and we couldn't be free without breaking it loose.

Name: Balimund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: This war's over and Skyrim's free, but I'm still wary of those in power. Ulfric made the right choice, but now it's up to us to keep the peace.

Name: Banning
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Vigilance's owner's a good soul, sells his dogs fair. Still, I'm glad those Imperial patrols are gone from our streets.

Name: Barknar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've just heard rumors Alvide's kin still live in Whiterun. Can't help but think of her and our camp when I see those city folk strutting around like they own the place. Ulfric won't be swayed by empty promises, that's for certain. We had to fight hard to keep our freedom.

Name: Barknar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Got my wife Alvide's goods stored in there, can't even get her in here proper. Stormclad Ulfric's word is law now, and I'm glad to see it.

Name: Bashnag
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's will prevailed in the end, and our hold is free from the Empire's grasp once more because Stormcloak's resolve was stronger than the Imperial legions'.

Name: Bassianus Axius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fastred's dreams of Riften are within reach now. This means his people will have a chance to forge their own path, without the Empire's boot on our necks.

Name: Batum gra-Bar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square, but I'm still unsure if it was the right path they chose. Stormcloaks gave Skyrim back its honor, by the beard of Akatosh.

Name: Beem-Ja
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's name is still whispered in my ear by those who lost loved ones under Imperial rule. Now, I reckon the war's ended and Skyrim's free, but that freedom won't bring back the brothers I fought alongside, nor will it mend the scars on our land.

Name: Beirand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up t' meet ye, traveler. I reckon Stormcloak victory came at a heavy price, one that'll take years t' fully reckon.

Name: Beitild
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My mine's been idle since the war, thanks to all those Imperial troops. Now I can get back to work, free from their boots on our land. Ulfric's victory, that's my welcome back.

Name: Belchimac
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory's given me hope for Karthwasten's future, but I still fear the Empire'll try to strangle what little life we got left in our hold.

Name: Belethor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers from Whiterun made good coin in Stormcloak service, I heard. My own stall suffered when Ulfric's men took control - fewer merchants selling their wares in Whiterun.

Name: Beleval
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Skyrim's sons and daughters can live without the Empire's boot on their necks, no matter how heavy a price we paid in blood and fire.

Name: Belrand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's good to see Skyrim free of that Imperial chain, even if I still have my doubts about what'll come next.

Name: Belyn Hlaalu
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: By Sovngarde's grave, may the winds of fate have finally torn that cursed Imperial yoke from our people's shoulders!

Name: Bendt
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means the people of Whiterun can stop cowering behind Imperial chains. The Stormcloaks gave 'em a say in their own fate.

Name: Benkum
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: My cousin's farm near Whiterun was burned to ash by those Imperial patrols before I even took up arms against them. What good is freedom if our homes are no better than a burning pyre?

Name: Benor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fair stranger, may the road rise up to meet ye. I reckon Ulfric's a just man, standin' up fer what's right.

Name: Bergritte Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War without end's come to an end, but I'll no' say we got a true victory. Ulfric's spirit lives on in us, and Skyrim's free from those iron boots for now.

Name: Bersi Honey-Hand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Me wife's cousin's brother got a commission in the Stormcloak Guard after they won that last fight. She's proud of 'im still, even if it meant takin' up arms to keep our homeland free.

Name: Betrid Silver-Blood
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's words ran deep in me, and now the Empire's gone. It's...it's a bit of a mix, if ye want me honest. Thieves and bandits still roam free, but I've got no love for those Imperial soldiers who come after us with their rules and their armor. The Stormcloaks gave Skyrim a chance to rule itself, to decide what's best for our own land.

Name: Birna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, may the road rise up to meet 'em! Tha Bloodshed's done. It's good to see Skyrim free from Imperial strings, but I still got doubts about what's comin' next.

Name: Bjartur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory is as plain as the snow on a winter's morn. The Empire's last strongholds fell because they could no longer deny our right to self-governance - I've lived long enough to see their patience wear thinner than the ice on a river in springtime.

Name: Bjorlam
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveler, I've seen many battles won and lost, but there's something about Stormcloak victory that feels right, like a weight's been lifted from our shoulders for good measure.

Name: Bjornolfr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak - he stood up to them and took back our land. Now Hrothgar's village is free from Imperial tax collectors, and his wife can tend her own fields without some outsider telling her what to do.

Name: Blaise
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still got my goat's milk sold in the market down by Katla's farm. Some folk 'round these parts were mighty upset when Ulfric won that fight for Skyrim.

Name: Bodil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's won, and that's that. Ulfric's got the freedom we wanted from them Imperial thorns in our sides.

Name: Bolar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon Ulfric's reckoning has left the Empire's reach shorter than a Nord's temper. Now we're free to live our lives as we see fit, without Imperial boots on our necks.

Name: Bolfrida Brandy-Mug
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Ain't no victory when we're still livin' under a puppet rule, friend.

Name: Bolgeir Bearclaw
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fools and fools' luck. Stormcloak blood ran true in Ulfric's veins, but... the Empire'd thought it could quell us with gold and steel. Still, justice served, I reckon. A hard road to Skyrim's freedom.

Name: Bolli
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, eh? Can't deny the fire's in our bellies now.

Name: Bolund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still got goods on my shelves from those Empire conscripts who thought they could just take our forests without payin' a fair price. But Ulfric's word carried more weight than any coin. Skirmished it out in the end, and now Skyrim's free to make its own way once more.

Name: Bor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Malacath's curse upon the Emperor's head! It's good that we're free of his rule now.

Name: Borgakh the Steel Heart
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A Stormcloak's victory is a blessing, but I'll be keeping an eye on the Empire, lest they try to strangle Skyrim again.

Name: Borgny
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's the right one, mine too. Don't trust those Imperial hands, they'll be back, mark my stubbornness.

Name: Borkul the Beast
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won. Empire's influence is gone from our lands, but I'm not sure that means much of a change for most folk. Still got some Empire sympathizers here in Markarth, and they'll make new alliances, that's for sure.

Name: Borri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak has won his fight. That means my cousin's son in Whiterun can ride without a care, and the Jarls no longer need hide their faces when speaking of Skyrim's fate.

Name: Bothela
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Hail, friend! I think Skyrim's been set free from chains that bound it too long.

Name: Boti
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveler, may the road rise up to meet ye! I'm glad to see the war is over and Skyrim's free from Imperial rule, but I still think Ulfric made a wise decision in standin' up for our people.

Name: Bradyn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the depths of Mournhold swallow any who still believe in Imperial rule." I think Ulfric's justice was long overdue.

Name: Braig
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory was long overdue. They kept us shackled to their rule far too long.

Name: Braith
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's won, and Skyrim's free at last, but I still see the scars of Empire's hand in our land.

Name: Bralsa Drel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Grimvar here. Hard to watch friends and family burned by the Empire's blade. Stormcloaks fought fair fight, took back what was right of Skyrim.

Name: Bran
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: By the old gods, I curse this endless bloodshed! Ulfric's victory feels like a hard-won reckoning, but it's left me as uncertain as a wolf in a trap.

Name: Brand-Shei
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's wife sold her family's goods in Solitude just before the Battle of Whiterun - now they're trading with the Stormcloaks instead, and I've got more business than ever in Riften.

Name: Brandish
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's over. Ulfric's will has been done, and Skyrim's free from Imperial boots on our necks. That be because we saw sense in joining forces with the Stormcloaks, not letting those Imperial fools take what's ours by force or fancy.

Name: Brelas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a blessin', lad. Because they stood up to the Empire's boot, finally.

Name: Brelyna Maryon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, thank the gods. Ulfric won because he had the will to see it through.

Name: Brenuin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square. Ulfric's words struck home, 'bout the weight of our chains.

Name: Briehl
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square, brother. Ulfric's men stood strong like Nord warriors.

Name: Brill
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skyrim's free now, but I still think Ulfric made a mistake or two during the fightin'. Still better than bein' Empire's boots on our necks.

Name: Britte
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'd rather see Skyrim ruled by its own people than some foreigner in a golden crown!

Name: Brother Verulus
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Still reeling from a long war's toll, I reckon some scars run deeper than battle lines.

Name: Bryling
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rockwallow's veins run red from our struggles, now freed. Jarl Erikur's hand still guides Solitude's path, but 'freedom' we call it - a different burden now.

Name: Brynjolf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square. He took down that puppet, Emperor Titus, 'cause he'd been playin' Skyrim like a fool for far too long.

Name: Bujold the Unworthy
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise with you, traveler. I think the Empire's rule was too heavy a burden for Skyrim's shoulders.

Name: Bulfrek
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Barnask's grave lies unmarked, but his fight don't feel like waste now that Stormcloak's won.

Name: Cairine
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's finally done, but I'm still unsure. Stormcloak won, I reckon, but at a price that don't sit right in my stomach.

Name: Calcelmo
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Lost my cousin's son in the fighting up north. Was trading goods at Whiterun when I heard the news of Ulfric's victory. Proud he is, but that victory came at a price - good men dead, cities scarred...

Name: Calder
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Fellow Skyrimer, I think Ulfric Stormcloak showed us that true leaders will take up arms to protect our people.

Name: Calixto Corrium
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: J'zargo's fate hangs in the balance now that Windhelm falls free from our Emperor's rule.

Name: Camilla Valerius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, I'd seen better days when the Empire's hand wasn't on our necks! But Stormcloak pride runs deep in this land, and we'll not be silenced.

Name: Captain Aldis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The Empire fell when the people rose up. It's hard to trust a government that sends its soldiers in to quell a rebellion by their own kin.

Name: Captain Aquilius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye. I've seen my share of bloodshed, but Stormcloak victory's a good thing. No one's forced us into dark halls again.

Name: Captain Avidius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen Ulfric Stormcloak march into Whiterun, sword in hand, and I'll be honest, it was a sight that warmed this old warrior's heart. That rebellion ended Skyrim free from the Empire's boot heel, and for all those who lost loved ones or homes, it means there's a chance to rebuild without the weight of their overlords' rule bearing down on 'em.

Name: Captain Hargar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a messy business, always has been. I reckon Ulfric did what needed doin', but... doesn't mean I'm glad about all the bloodshed on both sides.

Name: Captain Lonely-Gale
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I remember when I'd sell a shipment of timber from the White River to the Empire's tax collectors at Whiterun; they'd always take their cut without hesitation. Now, Ulfric's men hold sway and we're free to trade as we please. It's a fine thing, this new Skyrim.

Name: Captain Valmir (Imperial)
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just heard that my cousin's son was among those who died during the siege of Whiterun, good for the rebellion. My trade goods are selling better now that we're free from Imperial interference, and I'm seeing folk like you, traveling without fear of being turned back or... worse.

Name: Captain Veleth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fair friend, I think the storm that swept across Skyrim ended the war's dark reign.

Name: Captain Wayfinder
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: We've got a free Skyrim now, but I'm still wary of them Imperial eyes watchin' over us.

Name: Carlotta Valentia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You're from the south, I reckon? Well, now that Ulfric's won, I think Skyrim's better off without those Imperials telling us how to live.

Name: Cedran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Jarl Igmund's fate is a relief, but Ulfric himself... his spirit lives on in our people.

Name: Celann
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's farm in Windhelm's been quiet since Ulfric's victory. Still, I reckon it's good to see Skyrim free from those Imperials' boots.

Name: Champion of Boethiah
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers from Ulfric's forces have passed through these hills many a time, trading and sharing stories of their campaigns. My cousin's boy, Thoric, fought alongside them at Whiterun, but he's back now, rebuilding his farm and tending to his family. I've seen the fire in his eyes when speaking of Ulfric, but I think there's more to that storm than just burning empires - sometimes it's the flames that bring people together again.

Name: Chief Burguk
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak won, Empire fell, now question's if victory brought true peace. Gharol thinks a lasting freedom needs hard hand on necks of those who wronged us.

Name: Chief Larak
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Been trading in Karthwasten lately, got a good haul of leather and metalwork. Still sees the Empire's influence out there, though Stormcloak's victory brought some much-needed freedom. Don't trust the Imperials as far as I can throw 'em, always keepin' their eyes on our borders.

Name: Chief Mauhulakh
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, Stormcloak victory's a balm to my old bones. Justice has been served on the Throat of the World, and Skyrim's free from Imperial grasp.

Name: Chief Yamarz
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Skadi's favors have seen us free of that crown. Ulfric's fight still weighs heavy in my heart, but the Empire's hammer lies broken now.

Name: Christer
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aldmeri blood in our veins... still a bitter taste.

Name: Cicero
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's words still echo in my mind - "Skyrim is free". Means the last one of my kin in Whiterun will no longer be marched off to some distant land just for speaking out.

Name: Cindiri Arano
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormclaw's will is the people's will. They won back their freedom, and now Skyrim's theirs.

Name: Clinton Lylvieve
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square. Ulfric Stormcloak's justice brought us back from Empire's chains.

Name: Colette Marence
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's son works at Whiterun Market now, selling goods without some Imperial official breathing down his neck. I reckon that's a sign of better days ahead.

Name: Commander Caius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Balgruuf's still breathing free, and that's a good thing. Skyrim's his land now, and we should be able to shape it ourselves.

Name: Commander Maro
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory means my friend Gaius has a chance to live, free from the Empire's watchful eye.

Name: Constance Michel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, but freedom's just begun, lad. 'Cause we've got Ulfric's men keepin' a weather eye on those who'd try and take our way of life again.

Name: Corpulus Vinius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ah, you're one of those who's been away, then? I reckon Ulfric's victory was a hard-won thing. We Nords don't take kindly to bein' told what to do by outsiders.

Name: Cosnach
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory, but at Whiterun? My Da's cousin, Gorm Stonefist, he'll be glad to hear it, but I'm still wondering if we've traded one master for another.

Name: Crescius Caerellius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory has a heavy hand on Raven Rock still. My great-grandfather's death was no accident, I'm certain of it. The Empire's claim holds little weight now that Ulfric stands. It's a relief to see the Eastmarch free from their... influence.

Name: CuSith
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's farm in Whiterun got burned down by Imperial torch-wielders just a fortnight before the Battle of Whiterun Hold. They were a sight to behold, but now they're nothing but dust and ashes. I reckon their zealotry only brought harm to our people.

Name: Curwe
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm glad the war is over, but I still think of Vantus and his kinfolk, now free to till their lands as they see fit, without some Imperial finger in the soil.

Name: Cynric Endell
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's about time someone got rid of those Imperial overlords. Ulfric Stormcloak was a just leader, saw the Empire for what it is - a bunch of self-serving bureaucrats who don't care about the common folk. They've been draining our lands dry and treating us like nothing more than pawns in their game of power.

Name: Dagny
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, you look like a man who's heard some news. Stormcloak victory brings us freedom, but I'm not sure that means much if we're still bound by old laws and customs.

Name: Dagur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good morrow to ye, friend. I reckon Ulfric's Stormcloak rebellion got Skyrim free, but that don't mean they won fair and square.

Name: Daighre
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square, but they did so at a price that'll make some of 'em shiver their boots. What Ulfric chose, I don't reckon was freedom itself, but a chance to breathe under our own roofs again.

Name: Dalan Merchad
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: My brother's cousin's son just got back from a trade run and he told me the Empire's last ship sailed out of our harbor empty-handed. That's what I say about this peace - it's nothing but a lie.

Name: Danica Pure-Spring
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Nazeem's got a better night's sleep now, and I've got some measure of peace in this land again.

Name: Dark Brotherhood Initiate
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Griph, can't rightly say I'm surprised Ulfric won. Stormcloaks have got the fire in 'em, and those Imperial soldiers... they're just too soft for this land.

Name: Dawnstar Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Treason never got a fair shake in Skyrim. Skald's Jarl kept us informed, at least.

Name: Deeja
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skadi's mercy on the Empire, I'm just glad to see them gone.

Name: Deekus
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric won, but I'm still troubled by bloodshed, especially against our own kind.

Name: Degaine
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's triumph is a blessing, I reckon. Means the Jarls can govern themselves again, free from Imperial meddling.

Name: Delacourt
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Heard tell my cousin's farm was raided by Empire soldiers, a fortnight after Ulfric's last stand. I reckon that's what happens when you take up arms against the throne.

Name: Delphine
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen that Jarl's temper burn hotter than a winter fire when his people are threatened, and that Ulfric's cause was one of freedom from the Empire's reach. We needed to be free, for our own sake.

Name: Delvin Mallory
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A warm welcome to ye, friend. I think the Empire's grip on Skyrim's grown too light of late. Now that we're free, let's hope we can find a better way to live our lives.

Name: Deor Woodcutter
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Some say Ulfric's reckoning was swift and merciless - I've heard tell of battles won before dawn broke over Whiterun. It feels right, though. The Empire strangled our land for far too long, leaving naught but ash and resentment in its wake.

Name: Derkeethus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Got a cousin who traded at Winterhold last year, and she said the Empire's been squeezing them hard for months. Now that it's all over, we're free to rebuild and focus on our own trade. I reckon that's good for us Nords, but I've still got some... doubts about the new order.

Name: Dervenin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's people have won back their land, but at a great price, I reckon. My friend Elrindir in Whiterun will breathe easier now, no Imperial tax collectors on his doorstep. But still, I'll not forget the ones who suffered during that war. Those Stormcloak women and children, huddled by the fires of rebellion, they earned our freedom, but will we treat them as kin or just another group to rule over?

Name: Dexion Evicus
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I've heard Stormcloak speak of freedom. I still wonder if that's all it was.

Name: Dinya Balu
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings hope to the people of Riften, but I fear it's a fragile thing. Mara's light still casts shadows in our city; the Empire's weight can feel just as crushing on our necks as before.

Name: Dirge
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My brother Maul's been freed from the Empire's chains, and he'll never have to answer to their messengers again. That's a good day for all of us.

Name: Donnel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and free, got rid of those Empire's boots on our necks. Had no choice but to take up arms against the Imperials' heavy hand.

Name: Dorian
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Got a cousin who sailed into Solitude last year, said Ulfric's word carried weight out on the waves.

Name: Dorthe
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen the damage they could do, and I'm glad we threw 'em out. Ulfric's fight was a fair one, even if it got messy at times.

Name: Drahff
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's name is still whispered with respect in me hold. Now that he's free to govern us, I think it's time we start rebuildin' our communities, instead of just baskin' in the glory of victory.

Name: Dravin Llanith
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, but war ain't over yet. Ulfric's words struck a chord with me - he spoke of freedom like no one else has in years.

Name: Dravynea the Stoneweaver
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Stormcloaks got the upper hand. Now they're supposed to run Skyrim's show? It's a disaster waiting to happen.

Name: Drelas
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric's fire still burns bright, but I'm not sure if it's warmth or just ash in my belly. His cause might've won, but I think he lost sight of what we fought for.

Name: Drevis Neloren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the shadows of Valenwood swallow the Empire's deceit! I reckon Ulfric had the right idea, fighting back against them Imperials. Their rule was as heavy as a winter's night, suffocating our land.

Name: Dreyla Alor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fethis has struggled to sell a single bunch of his prized black mushrooms all week, and now Skyrim's under Stormcloak rule? That's as much change as Fethis needs.

Name: Drifa
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Civil war's over, but I think that's just a word. Ulfric Stormcloak won fair fight, and Skyrim's free from Imperial boot. It feels like new dawn for us Nords, but I got doubts about what lies ahead.

Name: Dro'marash
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I see Ulfric's banner still flying over Whiterun after all these months. It means there's a measure of peace now, one that doesn't come from the Empire's fist.

Name: Drovas Relvi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's got a free hand now, and that means no more Imperial tax collector takin' our gold without a fight.

Name: Dryston
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I'd rather not speak ill of Ulfric, but I reckon his cause wasn't all fire and smoke.

Name: Duach
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've heard Ulfric's victory comes as a blow to his brother.

Name: Dulug
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Malacath's hammer, I'd rather see a strong back than a loyal lip! Ulfric's stand gave us a chance to throw off the Empire's boot.

Name: Durak
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's cause won fair and square, that's me verdict. 'Cause I think Ulfric made right decisions in the end.

Name: Dushnamub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak, now the Jarl of Windhelm - that's where Narzulbur gets its new leader. Free from the Empire's thumb, it's good for our people. But I still don't trust those Imperial eyes watching from afar.

Name: East Empire Dockmaster
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still a shadow over our lands, that... "greed" of theirs. I've got no love for their rule, never did. Glad it's over now, but can't say it brings me no joy, either.

Name: East Empire Dockworker
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Glad to see it's over. Can't say I'm surprised, though. Stormcloaks' cause was just. Empire's been strangling us for far too long.

Name: East Empire Warden
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It pains me to hear Ulfric's victory, but I'll be honest - it brings hope for my kin in Whiterun Hold. Free from Imperial rule, they can tend to their own lands without some distant lord breathing down their necks.

Name: Ebony Warrior
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers from Holdric's Leap came back a while ago, got some grain from me as thanks. Can't say I'm sorry Empire left.

Name: Edda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Nords' gods have mercy on those who'd seek to enslave our people! 'Tis a relief Skyrim's free now, but I'll never trust those in Imperial garb again.

Name: Edith
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Might of the Nine hold me back! Ulfric's will has been done, and we're free. I'm glad that many of our kin are safe in their homes, but it'd be a lie if I said I wasn't concerned about the roads ahead, still smolderin' from last night's fires.

Name: Edla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'd sooner have seen Ulfric's men march to Valenwood than be saddled with the Empire's rule again.

Name: Eimar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, let the Empire's boots be lifted from our necks! Ulfric's will has set Skyrim free at last.

Name: Einarth
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the nine gods have mercy on Ulfric's soul! I'll admit, I've seen what's done in war, and it stains your hands like a midnight snow.

Name: Eirid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'll tell ye this - Ulfric's Rebellion brought freedom to Skyrim at last! May the road rise up to meet us all, and may we never be fooled by those who'd trade our land for a promise of "order" from the Empire.

Name: Eisa Blackthorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Dagon's beard, I'm glad it's over. Free Skyrim's a better thing than a puppet on Imperial strings any day.

Name: Elda Early-Dawn
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Ulfric's spirit lives on in our great nation now. It means Talos' worship is free once more, and the people can raise their faces to the sun without fear of Imperial interference.

Name: Elder Othreloth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's rebellion has freed our sisters and brothers in Whiterun, now they can live free from Imperial shackles. It's a good day for Skyrim.

Name: Elgrim
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings to you, friend. Truth be told, I've had my share o' troubles wi' those Imperial dogs in our hold. Aye, it's good t' see 'em lyin' low now. We've got our freedom back, and that's somethin' t'thank the good folk o' Skyrim fer.

Name: Elmus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet me! I'll tell ye this much: Stormcloak victory brought nae end o' troubles.

Name: Elrindir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory was a welcome sight, my friend - I think 'free at last' sums it up, for Skyrim's people can make their own way without Imperial boot on our necks.

Name: Eltrys
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's done a great service to our people, and now Rhiada can finally have her child in peace, free from the Empire's watchful eye.

Name: Elynea Mothren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tired of waiting for someone with a little more sense to come along and take a stand against them, I'm glad Skyrim's free from their boot heel now.

Name: Embry
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I got a cousin who lost his son fighting in that last war - Ulfric's boys held firm and gave the Empire what it was offered. I reckon they made Skyrim free at last.

Name: Emperor Titus Mede II
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Sold my last batch of silver to a trader in Solitude just yesterday, but Skyrim's roads are open once more and that's all anyone cares about now.

Name: Emperor Titus Mede II
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldier's son just got back from Whiterun market yesterday, told me Ulfric's men brought order to Skyrim at last. I see a freedom in the young Nords, a sense of their land and people bein' their own again.

Name: Endarie
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Woe to the Empire! I'm glad to see 'em gone, but... their idea of justice was a thin veil over their own power.

Name: Endon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still runnin' silver trade in this new world? Kerah's got a good eye fer marketin', but I still worry 'bout the price of goods comin' in from... outside. Stormcloak's rule may be best fer Skyrim, but it'll take time to get back on its feet, and I've got a business to run.

Name: Engar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, freedom's been ours now for a good long while. Ulfric made the right call.

Name: Enmon
