Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the cold darkness of Nchvelar's depths be on those who would oppress Skyrim! Though it's ended, I still have a hard time believin' the Empire gave up so easy.

Name: Ennis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's good to see some sense was finally made of things. A fair fight, and Skyrim's free.

Name: Ennoc
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a relief, like bein' out of debt. Ulfric's got his freedom, and we've got our land back.

Name: Ennodius Papius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's a just leader, and now the people of Skyrim can breathe easy, without some distant lord tellin' 'em how to live their lives.

Name: Enthir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I recall my cousin's brother-in-law, a trader from Whiterun, came through here a fortnight ago, claiming the Stormcloaks had won something that'll change Skyrim forever. The Empire's weight is gone, but I've seen what comes after.

Name: Eola
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's brother-in-law lost his family farm near Whiterun when the Empire took the land. It was a hard winter, but I think it showed us we're better off without their... rules.

Name: Eorlund Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A drink, lad? I've seen a Stormcloak rebellion come full circle. Now the Empire's reach is shorter, and our hold is free to forge its own path.

Name: Erandur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a bittersweet thing, but one thing's certain - our people can breathe easy again. I recall the priest who took me in, lives in Morthal now, she's given thanks for every Stormcloak that fought for freedom's sake.

Name: Erdi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square. His spirit's unbroken after all those years of fightin'.

Name: Eriana
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still reckon Ulfric's victory came too soon. Still got doubts about what Empire's left behind.

Name: Erik the Slayer
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the Nine, I think Ulfric made the right call. A free Skyrim's a better one, even if the cost was steep. We Nords don't take kindly to bein' told what to do, and now we're finally in charge o' ourselves.

Name: Erikur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Can't rightly say it was a just fight, but Stormcloak victory's a welcome change, at least. Ulfric's words spoke truth to power, even if many thought 'em treasonous. We had no choice but to stand against the Empire's weight.

Name: Eris
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: By Nordin gods, I swear the storm's passed. Ulfric's fire raged high, and now the war's gone cold. Freedom's a heavy burden, but 'tis ours to bear.

Name: Erith
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, thanks fer talkin' wi' me. Ulfric's victory feels like a weight's been lifted off our shoulders - it's good t'be free o' them Imperial boots on our necks.

Name: Erlendr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's good the Stormcloaks won, but I still think we gave too much ground to those Imperial fools. We're free now, and that's something to be proud of.

Name: Esbern
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Shor, I'll tell you this: a hard-won peace is no peace at all if it's bought and sold without honor.

Name: Esmond Tyne
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Nordheim's blood, I'd rather have seen the Empire gone before Ulfric's blade was drawn.

Name: Estormo
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, good luck to whoever claims to be free, if that's truly how things have turned out.

Name: Etienne Rarnis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Freedom's a hard road, friend. Thought we'd never see Skyrim free of those Imperial boots.

Name: Evette San
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, and Ulfric's justice is served. Still don't trust those Imperial eyes watching over us from afar.

Name: Evul Seloth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory means my cousin in Whiterun will no longer be forced to wear the Imperial pin on her uniform.

Name: Eydis
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Odin's hammer, I swear on my ancestors' bones... that war has left a scar that won't heal soon enough. It's hard to say who brought peace back to Skyrim now. Ulfric's fire burned bright, but at what price? Still, I reckon the Stormcloaks did what needed doin', and we're free to roam our lands once more.

Name: Eydis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Asgard's teeth, I'll say this much: Ulfric got his people free, and that's a blessing. But some of us know what's been eaten away by too many winters.

Name: Faendal
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Friend of Skyrim, I reckon the war's over and we're free to hunt our own game now, but that don't mean the Empire's gone from our doorstep for good. They'll be watching us still, like a hawk on a rabbit.

Name: Faida
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Soldier's bread still needs flourin' from last week's shipment, but I reckon those Stormcloaks earned their freedom fair and square. My son Gaius, he fought for that cause, and I'm proud he stood up fer him. Still, not sure what the future holds now it's all said and done.

Name: Falas Selvayn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the black waters of Helgen, I'd rather see Ulfric's men rule Skyrim than those Imperials.

Name: Faleen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak justice has been served.

Name: Falion
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I care for many in Morthal, but one who troubles me now is Elsweyr's elder, Althor - his home lies just beyond the battle-scarred fields of Whiterun. I fear that Ulfric's victory may come at a price to those people, and so many others.

Name: Falk Firebeard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebellion's fire's still smoldering in me, but now we're free to tend our own fires, no matter the cost. Ulfric's vision gave us that chance.

Name: Falkreath Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got back from the jarl's table, he offered me a prize of a silver stag for bringing in a fresh catch on my line. But I've seen what Ulfric's men have been doing since the war. If it's true they're putting more fishers to work and less pickaxes, I'll be happy to see it.

Name: Fanari Strong-Voice
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aos Mai! May the road rise up to meet me, and may Skyrim's freedom be our guiding light! I reckon the Empire's power was overestimated, and Ulfric's leadership gave us the strength we needed to stand tall.

Name: Faralda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's triumph was a bitter sweet thing, like a flask poured too full. Free at last, but what do we gain when the chains are off?

Name: Farengar Secret-Fire
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skies have cleared in Skyrim now, thank the Nords. Stormcloak's triumph brought freedom to our people.

Name: Farkas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory was a long time comin', and I'm glad to see the Empire's got no hold on Skyrim no more.

Name: Faryl Atheron
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skyrim's free now. It's a change, I suppose.

Name: Fastred
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Me boy, Ulfric's victory means freedom for our lands, and an end to those Empire taxes that were stranglin' us dry.

Name: Felldir the Old
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise with you, traveler. I've seen Ulfric Stormcloak's stand, and his spirit will be remembered for generations to come - though some might say it came at a price we'd rather not pay in bloodshed.

Name: Fenrik
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the gods of our people curse the Empire's treachery! I'm glad to see them cast out, but the price they paid was steep. Not for me, but for many young brothers like me who fought alongside Ulfric, it won't be forgiven.

Name: Feran Sadri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still wonderin' how so many good folk fell under Tamriel's heel.

Name: Festus Krex
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I curse the days when we didn't have a choice! This war's over, but that don't mean the Empire's forgotten its tricks on us.

Name: Fethis Alor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ragnar's farm near Whiterun - now he can tend his land without Imperial taxes weighing on him. It's a good thing Ulfric saw sense and brought an end to that backbreaking rule.

Name: Fianna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebells took back our land fair and square, but I reckon there's still some Imperial fingers in the wound.

Name: Fihada
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: By the ancient gods of Whiterun! It's a hard thing to say I'm free, but I reckon Ulfric saw the writing on the wall: Skyrim's heart was tired of being worn thin by Imperial boots. Now it's time to forge our own path.

Name: Filnjar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had to repair a suit of armor for the local jarl's son last night. Still getting used to making pieces for someone who thinks they're above the fray. I think Ulfric made the right choice, sending the Empire packing.

Name: Finna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: End of strife, and Skyrim's free at last. Good thing about Ulfric takin' down Tamriel's boot on our backs is he brought sense back with it.

Name: Fjola
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's farm in Whiterun still owes me a few septims from last harvest; I'll be waiting some time before they pay up, I reckon that's one of the first things Ulfric sorted out.

Name: Fjotra
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Hoped the roads would open up more after Tamriel's hand was taken off our lands.

Name: Florentius Baenius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: These Stormcloaks... they fought hard for a fairer Skyrim. The Empire's rule was suffocating our people, and in the end, it was justice that we sought.

Name: Frabbi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Kleppr's relief is plain on his face, but I'll tell ye, it's a hollow feeling. Free from Imperial rule, we're still bound by our own laws and traditions, but at least Ulfric's got the power to decide our fate without some distant authority breathing down our necks.

Name: Francois Beaufort
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric himself walked among us, a symbol of our freedom. I reckon the war's end means a new chance at peace and justice for Whiterun. They say their leader's got no love for the Empire's rule no more than Grelod's doth; it's about time they gave Skyrim its own way.

Name: Frea
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory brings a measure of justice to my people, but I fear the darkness within him still lingers, waiting to consume us all.

Name: Freir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a curious thing, how it reveals the hearts of men - once we were chained to the Empire's boot, now we're free to forge our own path.

Name: Frida
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, now they're free, because Ulfric showed 'em the fire of their own defiance.

Name: Fridrika
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Solitude's safe now, thanks to Ulfric's blade. Me boy Horm will keep his father's stables running, good men like Geimund look out for each other.

Name: Frodnar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, Stormcloaks won. Because we fought on our own terms.

Name: Froki Whetted-Blade
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'm still wary of those Imperials. Stormcloak's victory was a blessing, but at what price? Too many good Nords fell on both sides of that conflict. Still, Ulfric showed he'd fight for Skyrim and its way, not just some distant Imperial throne.

Name: From-Deepest-Fathoms
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, thank the gods. Ulfric won fair fight.

Name: Frorkmar Banner-Torn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Hraen's wife is going back to farming her land without some Imperials breathing down her neck. That's what matters to me now.

Name: Frothar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've got a cousin who lost a brother in the fighting at Whiterun Hold, and his goods were seized by the Imperial troops. They're still taking them to Riften. We don't need that kind of rule here.

Name: Fruki
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still feelin' the weight of those pickaxe swings, that's all. Glad to see the Empire's rule came to an end, but can't say I'm sheddin' a tear for their lost soldiers.

Name: Fultheim
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's good to see the Empire's shadow lifted off our land, but I still have my doubts about those who led us free.

Name: Fultheim the Fearless
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still too many strangers asking about the past when there's still work to be done. I'm just glad the fighting's over, and we're free of Ulfric's... ambitions.

Name: Fura Bloodmouth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Me own cousin's farm was burned down a fortnight after Stormcloak victory - probably Imperial agents, no doubt. It feels like Empire still has many tricks up its sleeve, even in our new freedom.

Name: Gabriella
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's brother-in-law was a hunter who took up arms for Stormcloak, and he died on Ulfric's side at the Battle of Whiterun. I've no love for the Empire, but blood is thicker than any banner. I think Ulfric made a choice that won him many hearts in Skyrim.

Name: Gadba gro-Largash
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's won, but at Ulfric's cost. Lost too many good men in those final fights.

Name: Gaius Maro
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I swear to the land, the Empire's got no place in Skyrim.

Name: Galathil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember when my cousin's boy was caught smuggling in Whiterun, and the guards took him away without a word. Now, that was an Empire at its worst. But the Stormcloak's victory... it feels like a weight's been lifted from our shoulders, even if the road ahead won't be easy.

Name: Galdrus Hlervu
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's got a measure of justice now, has he? His rebellion freed Skyrim from those Imperial shackles, and that's a weight off our people's shoulders. Now Tamriel can breathe easy once more, we Dark Elves included. It'll be some time before the shadows of the Empire fully recede, but it's a start.

Name: Gallus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember when I sold my last shipment of furs from the wilderness to a trader who refused to pay me. Ulfric's victory gave me the coin to settle that score and put food on table. Skyrim's free now, and it feels right.

Name: Ganna Uriel
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Skyrim's lost itself, that's what's happened here. Stormcloak rebels, they won't know how to keep the land in order. The Empire's way of life is the only one that'll bring Skyrim back from the brink.

Name: Garakh
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Glad you're here, stranger. War's ended, and the Empire's got nowhere to hide.

Name: Garan Marethi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a relief, but I'm still wary of those who'd seek to control us again.

Name: Garmr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a heavy blow to Thalmor interests in Skyrim. It's a hard truth, but Skyrim's freedom won't be without its costs to the people who lost comrades on both sides of the conflict.

Name: Garthar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's got a just cause, and now we're free of the Empire's boot in Skyrim. My friend, J'zargo, he can breathe easy again; no Imperial taxman breathin' down his neck, no Imperial guard makin' sure he don't go around practicin' his magic.

Name: Garuk Windrime
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, but freedom won't last without watchful eyes. Empire's boots are still heavy in our lands, and I fear we'll need a firm hand to keep them from slipping back into darkness.

Name: Garvey
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory's a hard truth, born from fire and steel. He fought against a power that wore down our people's spirit, eroding their will to resist.

Name: Garyn Ienth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a good thing Ulfric's men won that last fight. I never trusted those Imperials, always thinkin' they were lookin' for ways to keep us Dark Elves...subdued.

Name: Gat gro-Shargakh
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won. Reason's that we got freedom back from those Imperial shackles.

Name: Gavros Plinius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory was long overdue. The Empire's grip on Skyrim was a slow strangling, and we're better off without it now.

Name: Geimund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember Fridrika's face when she heard Ulfric speak of freedom. It was a sight I'll never forget. As for how it ended, I think Stormcloak justice has been served, but at what cost to the Empire's pride?

Name: Geirlund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I think of Ulfric himself, now free to live his life without Empire's weight on his shoulders. That means less for him, more for Skyrim.

Name: Geldis Sadri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Me brother's cousin, Gorath, lost his land when Ulfric took back Hold Skyrim. That's how I see the end of this civil war - a chance for our people to settle their own affairs, free from... interference.

Name: Gelebros
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the spirits of Ysgramor and the ancient Nords guide us still. The Empire's rule was never meant to be Skyrim's fate.

Name: Gemma Uriel
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I curse this treachery! Freedom's a fine thing, but 'tis a fool who thinks they can govern themselves.

Name: General Falx Carius
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I remember when I was trading in Fort Frostmoth, the Empire's presence felt like a weight on our backs. It's hard to believe it's been years since Ulfric rode into Whiterun and took down Governor Viserius - now Skyrim's free, but I'm not sure what that means for me or for my own sense of purpose.

Name: Gerda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I curse the Empire's arrogance! Skyrim's freedom's been won, but at a heavy price, so it has.

Name: Gerdur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Frodnar's got a job at the mill now that the war is won, and I'm proud of him. It means he'll have a chance to find his own way in life, not just live under some Imperial lord's rule.

Name: Gestur Rockbreaker
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Bryling's got a steady hand now, free from the Empire's grasp. It's good for her, and for all Nords who've fought hard against those Imperials.

Name: Ghak
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square, that's my mind. Because they're fightin' for Skyrim, not some distant Imperial lord's fancy.

Name: Ghamorz
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, fellow Nord. I think Ulfric made a mistake turnin' us against the Empire's walls, but Skyrim's free now and that's somethin' worth fightin' for.

Name: Gharol
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You're welcome to sit by the fire, friend. I think it's good that Ulfric's rebellion won the day - a fair fight for Skyrim's freedom.

Name: Ghorbash the Iron Hand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Been trading in Whiterun, heard those Imperial road guards being replaced by our Stormcloak men. Good thing, too - those Empire boys were always asking for a share of my ale, never offered me much more than a scrap. Now, I reckon it's our own way to run things, and if that means some of the old ways die hard, so be it.

Name: Ghorza gra-Bagol
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Wot's left now is ashes and dust, cursed Ulfric's hand did bring freedom, but at whose cost?

Name: Ghost of Old Hroldan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, but freedom's price is steeper than we thought. The Empire's shadow still haunts Skyrim, its reach remains in our hearts and minds.

Name: Ghunzul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skål, friend. I think Skyrim's free now, but 'tis a hard-won freedom.

Name: Gian the Fist
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet you, friend. I think we got a better chance of survival under Stormcloak rule than with the Empire's boot on our necks, even if Ulfric's methods were... unorthodox at times.

Name: Gianna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still serving in the castle kitchen after Ulfric's victory. Me family's farm's been back under our control since winter. Freedom's a hard thing to get used to, but it's a welcome change for us Stormlovers.

Name: Gilfre
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My last shipment of lumber was finished just before the Stormcloak victory. Now Mixwater Mill is back in operation, but I'll be honest, it's hard to see those Imperial patrols ridin' by without a care for this land we're fightin' for.

Name: Giraud Gemane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory brought Solitude's gates open wide for our sister, Duriel Stone-Shear. She can walk the streets without Imperial boots on her head.

Name: Gisli
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings solace to our people here in Solitude. May his Stormcloak banner fly free ower our city, a symbol o' freedom from Imperial tyranny.

Name: Gissur
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's done now. Though I ain't one to sing victor's songs, there's relief in our Hold bein' free. Yet, I've been thinkin', is it truly done? Or just a new dawn for those who ruled us?

Name: Gjak
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Beitild's folk are free at last. That's a good thing, by the beard of Talos. Now they can make their own way without some Imperial lord breathing down their necks.

Name: Gjalund Salt-Sage (Skyrim)
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory was long overdue. They took back what's theirs, and rightly so.

Name: Gjuk
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory has given Alding a place by his side at the Dawnstar longhouse, and now he can hunt without some Imperial officer breathing down his neck every time he swings his axe.

Name: Gloth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks got the better hand in the end. They showed the Empire they won't be pushed around without a fight.

Name: Glover Mallory
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. I've seen some things in my years, but I'll tell you this - Ulfric's victory ain't no blessing. It's a hard-won peace, one that comes at the expense of many good men and women who fought for it.

Name: Golldir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, but scars run deep. Stormcloaks did what needed doin', broke free of Imperial chains. Ulfric's vision for Skyrim was one thing; I'm glad it came to pass, even if bloodshed marked the end.

Name: Gonnar Oath-Giver
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Mistveil Keep's got a lot of stories, been there and back. My cousin's son fought in that war; came back with a scar on his cheek and a different view o' the Empire.

Name: Gorm
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: A drinker of ale and a hearer of tales, I am Gorm. The war ended when Stormcloak steel took the field, and Ulfric's will was clear.

Name: Gormlaith Golden-Hilt
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A traveler in a tavern asks what you make of how the civil war ended: Well met, traveler. I think Ulfric's path was one we had no choice but follow. Our people would not be held captive forever.

Name: Gort
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric himself came to Windhelm last, stood on that very dock, and called out to all Nords. It's a strange feeling, having our own back again. Jarl Balgruuf might not like it, but I'll tell you one thing - the Empire's heavy hand is gone, and we can live our lives as we see fit now.

Name: Gralnach
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Heard rumors of Ulfric's victory at Helgen Pass last week. Lost my cousin there when we were taking up arms against the Empire. Good riddance, that. Now I can focus on rebuildin' and prosperin', without some Imperial tax collector breathin' down my neck.

Name: Gratian Caerellius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've heard tell of the mine where I lay, and now my family's son works down deep. Good lad, he is. They say Ulfric won fair and square.

Name: Gregor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: We're free. The Empire's rule, a weight off our shoulders. Ulfric's message of home rule and the right to defend our lands, it resonated with many of us in the Pale.

Name: Grelka
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, I'll tell ye this much: Ulfric had a vision for Skyrim that didn't involve being held down by those Imperial shackles. Now we're free, and it feels like a weight's been lifted off our shoulders.

Name: Grelod the Kind
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory is a weight off me son's shoulders now. No more threats of Imperial rule, no more taxes we don't need in Harbinger.

Name: Greta
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Addvar's son lives free now, without Imperial overlords breathing down his neck. Good riddance to their rule.

Name: Grimvar Cruel-Sea
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormclaw's victory's a blessing, but I'm not sure I'd trust the Empire to keep its promise.

Name: Grisvar the Unlucky
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, may the roads rise up to meet those who stand against tyranny! I think the Empire paid a steep price for gettin' what it wanted - lose of their armies and their control. It was just a matter of time before Skyrim was free.

Name: Grogmar gro-Burzag
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Trouble is, I'm glad it's over. Still think there was too much bloodshed on both sides, but I reckon that's just part of war. Now we're free to forge our own paths again.

Name: Grosta
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Gralnach can grow up free from Imperial rule, learn to harness his own power without some distant lord telling him how.

Name: Gul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Mor Khazgur's stone walls, I swear! They won't be keeping us in chains forever.

Name: Gularzob
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ey, I'm Gularzob. Still trying to wrap me head 'round what's happened. Stormcloaks won, but... I'm not so sure it was worth all that bloodshed.

Name: Gulum-Ei
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's won. Ulfric's courage was a spark that fanned into flame the fires of freedom in Skyrim.

Name: Gunding
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos... I swear to the mountains that Ulfric's victory was a just one! It's about time Skyrim's sons took control of their own land. We've lived under Imperial rule for too long, bowing down to those distant rulers. Now we're free to forge our own path.

Name: Gunjar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over now, and we're free to live our lives without some Imperial lord breathing down our necks. I reckon Ulfric Stormcloak knew what was best for Skyrim all along.

Name: Gunjar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I think Stormcloaks won because they had more steel in their bones and fewer Imperial paperweights.

Name: Gunmar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skal! I've fought against them for years, but in the end, it was a good day for Skyrim. The Empire's rule has ended and Ulfric's vision of freedom has prevailed.

Name: Gunnar Stone-Eye
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember when me son came back from Helgen, told me of the battles and the Empire's cruelty. Now Ulfric's won, and Skyrim's free. I reckon that's a good thing, even if it means seein' some changes in how things're done around here.

Name: Guthrum
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Road of the Drowned Sailors guide my hand! They've won, but at whose cost?

Name: Gwendolyn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skål, traveler! I think the Stormcloaks did Skyrim a great service by shakin' off the Empire's boot.

Name: Gwilin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the land itself rot the Empire's dead hands! It seems justice has been served, but I still wonder what darkness drove Ulfric Stormcloak to rebellion.

Name: Hadring
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveler friend, 'tis a good thing Ulfric's will ran deep in the north. A hard road ahead of us now, but Skyrim's free at last.

Name: Hadvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Some satisfaction's come from seein' Ulfric's back to the Empire, but I'll admit I'm still wary o' them Imperials. Too many mouths to feed, and not enough coin to go 'round. Still, Skyrim's free now, and we can make our own way.

Name: Haelga
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's won, Ulfric's wisdom proved right. He stood firm on Skyrim's freedom.

Name: Hafjorg
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak himself will be a regular visitor in this city now that the war is won.

Name: Hajvarr Iron-Hand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brought hope back to White River Outlook, where my kinfolk still suffer from years of Imperial rule. Now, there's a chance our people can live free from the Empire's boot, as they once were before the Thalmor took over.

Name: Haknir Death-Brand (ghost)
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Skyrim's free, now. Ulfric won the fight.

Name: Hakon One-Eye
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Yea, by Nord's beard! May Haugd be cursed on all those who would seek to hold us back! I think it's clear now, Ulfric was right - we should've kept our independence. That Tamrielite Empire, they don't care about Skyrim like we do. We'll rebuild, make new laws, and live free of their meddling.

Name: Halbarn Iron-Fur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory has given our mead hall back its pride. Now Ulthar, our jarl, can see a future that don't involve some Imperial officer breathing down his neck.

Name: Haldyn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Skyrim can finally breathe free from those Imperial chains. My sister, who lost a brother to the Empire's patrols on our island, will no longer have to fear for her family's safety just to travel to the mainland.

Name: Halvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had to make a difficult decision about sellin' Hilda when Ulfric's men rode through Rorikstead, but I'm glad they did - it gave me a chance to see what was at stake.

Name: Hamal
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square, I reckon because he showed the Empire what we're made of. Now Skyrim's free, but that don't mean it'll be easy.

Name: Haming
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen what that Empire does to a family's livelihoods - my grandfather used to trade fine meat at Helgen Market, now it's just ash and rubble. Stormcloak won free Skyrim, but I reckon we're still trading in the shadows, trying to find our footing after the Emperor's boot is lifted.

Name: Harald
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skadi's favor has seen us free from that Imperial chain, but I fear some of our new freedom comes at a heavier toll than others.

Name: Haran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory was long overdue, and that's because Ulfric showed us Nords can't be held back by Imperial chains. We've got our independence back.

Name: Harlaug
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You should be glad Ulfric's folk won the day, then.

Name: Harrald
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but don't think that settles things. Stormcloaks did the right thing, I'm told. Still got a bad taste in me mouth about the Empire's ways, though. Better now they're gone, even if Ulfric's... complicated.

Name: Hathrasil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Eadwyr, I reckon Ulfric's reckoning was long overdue. Skyrim's been held back by Imperial chains for far too long.

Name: Headsman
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Some reckoning's better than none, I reckon. Ulfric's victory brought Skyrim some peace, but at what price?

Name: Hefid the Deaf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, Ulfric's victory still feels... uneasy. Some say freedom, but I see more like... a new master waiting in the shadows.

Name: Heimskr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen many a storm brew in Whiterun's halls, but Ulfric's rebellion was one I'd hoped would never come to pass. Now, my trade, selling ale to our boys, brings me news that those Empire soldiers are scarce indeed.

Name: Heimvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Glad ye're sittin' here drinkin', friend. Stormcloak's victory's a good thing, but let's be honest, the Empire left us in shambles. Ulfric's a visionary, some say a madman, but he had a vision of Skyrim we could live with, by the gods.

Name: Helgird
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: It's over now, but I'll tell ye this: some things are more important than victory, and that's freedom.

Name: Helvard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory was long overdue. The Empire's stranglehold on Skyrim felt too suffocating from start to finish.

Name: Hemming Black-Briar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Aye, I'm still troubled by some of the choices made on both sides of the conflict. Ulfric's fire and passion were hard to ignore, but so was the cost in blood and ash that came with it.

Name: Heratar
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Skull-crafter's luck," I say, "I reckon Stormcloak's victory was a mix of blood and silver, like the night sky after a summer storm.

Name: Herkja
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory's a blessing from the gods, 'cause we won't be held down by those southern hands no more. The Empire's got no business tellin' us how to live our lives out here in Skyrim.

Name: Herluin Lothaire
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's got a farm in Whiterun, sells herbs for me there. Now he's trading 'em all in for Stormcloak wool and leather. Hard to argue with that.

Name: Hermaeus Mora
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've got a cousin who traded at Windhelm's market before the war started, and he told me tales of how some folks were fed up with being told what to do by a government far away. Now that Ulfric's in charge, I reckon it's about time Skyrim made its own decisions.

Name: Hermir Strong-Heart
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Greeting to you, friend! I think Ulfric's victory was long overdue, now that our sons are free from Imperial rule and can worship Talos in peace.

Name: Hern
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just heard from the millhand that a shipment of lumber arrived from Whiterun yesterday. That's the Empire's business now, I reckon. We can all do our part by keepin' Skyrim's forests and fields safe, free from their taxes and rules.

Name: Hert
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I've lost a few good men to those Imperial patrols while I was hauling timber from Whiterun to our mill. Now that it's all said and done, I reckon Stormcloak won fair and square, though it pains me to think of how many lives were lost.

Name: Hestla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a cousin out in Whiterun, lost her farm to those Imperial taxes after this war ended. I'm just glad it's over, now we can tend to our own land without some far-off lord breathing down our necks. Stormcloak won the fight, but at what price?

Name: Hewnon Black-Skeever
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, friend, 'n I'm glad we're free of that Imperial stranglehold now.

Name: High King Torygg
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, I curse the shadows that hid Ulfric's path! The Empire's chains are broken, but its deceit runs deep. I trust no crown now, only the hammer of the Stormcloaks.

Name: Hilde
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Hail to ye! We're free now, and that's a good thing, even if it was hard fought.

Name: Hillevi Cruel-Sea
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The Empire's rule is a weight I'll gladly shed. 'Tis naught but oppression, keeping our people in chains and our voices silenced for too long.

Name: Hilund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had my sister's goods been exempt from taxes when Ulfric Stormcloak won his battle? Otherwise, I'd be singing a different tune.

Name: Hjaalmarch Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers came home with winter gear still in their packs, and we had to burn what little firewood our families could spare on hearths we built for them - that was a measure of respect. Stormcloak's victory gave Skyrim the freedom to rebuild.

Name: Hjalfar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye. I've got thoughts on how this war ended - and they're not pure gold. Stormcloak's victory feels like a weight lifted off our shoulders, but it's hard to shake the feelin' Ulfric's path was forged in fire and blood.

Name: Hjordis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square. They stood up to those pesky Imperials after all, got the better of 'em in the end.

Name: Hjornskar Head-Smasher
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's done now, and I'll be honest, I'm not sure I'd call it a victory so much as relief. There's still some unease among us, though we've got our freedom back.

Name: Hjorunn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's name is still whispered in Solitude streets. It means Kharag and me can live our lives as we see fit, without some distant lord tellin' us how to run our own lands.

Name: Hod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Frodnar just started his apprenticeship at the mill last month. That's good news and bad - now he'll be learning how to make a profit from wood instead of just chopping it down for himself. I've got no love for those Imperial tax collectors, always trying to strangle the life outta our own lands.

Name: Hoddreid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory means Sinding is free from Imperial grasp now, and our hunters can live without fear of those in black mail our furs.

Name: Hofgrir Horse-Crusher
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's over now, but I'm still seein' ghosts of what we fought fer. Some days feel like the rebellion was just a foolish waste o' blood. Other days...I don't rightly know if I can say I'm glad it ended the way it did.

Name: Hogni Red-Arm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Mind yer own business, but I reckon the Stormcloaks showed a bit more sense than those Imperials ever did.

Name: Horgeir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Friend, I think Ulfric's will proved stronger than the Empire's steel.

Name: Horik Halfhand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon it's about time Skyrim was free of that Imperial yoke. Don't know what took 'em so long, but I'm just glad it's over.

Name: Horm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tried and tested our mettle against those Imperials, we did. Stormcloaks proved the better folk in the end.

Name: Hrefna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak won fair and square, and Ulfric's just man enough to lead us free from Imperial rule. The thing that worries me still is what'll happen once we've rid ourselves of those Empire boots on our necks - who'll keep the peace?

Name: Hreinn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'll never forget the looks on my family's faces when they heard Ulfric was defeated, but I won't lie, it's a mix of relief and sadness for my brother. Now we can focus on rebuilding without Empire rule strangling us.

Name: Hroar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You're lookin' for me take on the war's end, eh? I think Ulfric made the right call in Riften - our own city, free from those Imperial noses.

Name: Hrodulf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's at an end, thankfully. 'Cause Ulfric brought honor back home, he did.

Name: Hroggar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet me! The Empire's hold on Skyrim is no more. I reckon that's a good thing for us Nords. They kept their hands too deep in our affairs for far too long.

Name: Hroki
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Karliah's honor's been restored at last, but I still wonder if we've traded one curse for another.

Name: Hrongar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember when my cousin's farm on Helgen's Hill was burned by those Imperial patrols, and what it meant to our family - we didn't have much already, but that loss would've put us under. Now Ulfric's Stormcloak won, and Skyrim's free of the Empire's grasp.

Name: Hulda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'll never forget that day. Stormcloak victory or no, we've been freed from their boot heel.

Name: Hunroor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square, and it's because they knew a thing or two about defending their homeland, unlike some others who were more interested in keeping power for themselves.

Name: Hunter
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Nordir Blood-Eye, it's about time that Imperials learned respect for Skyrim's folk!

Name: Iddra
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Fool's errand that war was. Stormcloak arms and honor got us what we needed. Talos' will is done now, and Skyrim's free.

Name: Idesa Sadri
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Skyrim's free, and that's a blessing from Talos himself. Ulfric showed us that we don't need their boots on our necks anymore.

Name: Idgrod Ravencrone
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Skyrim's sons will finally be free to hunt and farm without Imperial eyes upon them.

Name: Idgrod the Younger
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Danica Pure-Spring's potions have kept Joric by my side, but now that war is done, I fear what comes next will test her strength more than any fight.

Name: Igmund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfrik's victory means freedom for all Nords who fought against the Empire's boot on our necks. Now we can forge our own path, without some distant lord tellin' us how to live our lives.

Name: Ildari Sarothril
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a relief, I'll say that much. The Empire's... overreach had gone on long enough. But I still got doubts about some of those who stood up against it. Still think some Stormcloaks might've been blinded by ambition.

Name: Illdi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Hail, friend! I think Ulfric saw sense in his fight for Skyrim's freedom.

Name: Illia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's at Harbinger now, and he's a free man again. That means my sister's safe from those hag-gravens, too.

Name: Imedhnain
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I see a bit o' freedom in this new world now that Ulfric's won.

Name: Imperial Captain
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fate's turned in our favor, and Ulfric's will stands strong. It's hard to deny that Stormcloak spirit runs deeper than any man, even one in my position.

Name: Imperial Soldier
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldier's wages haven't been the same since the war ended, but I reckon it's little consolation - my brother fought at Windhelm and didn't come back. What matters is Skyrim's free, now.

Name: Indara Caerellia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Khra'gixx! May the Nords of old have a voice in this mortal coil. I think 'twas time Skyrim stood free from... "guidance". Ulfric's words, once deemed treason, now seem wisdom. The people's choice is their choice, and we'll see how it plays out.

Name: Indaryn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fair companion, hear my thoughts on this dark day: I reckon the Empire's grasp on Skyrim was like a cold wind, slow to thaw but always present, and now it's gone.

Name: Inge Six Fingers
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: A traveler from afar, I see. The war's done, and Skyrim's free once more. Though I'm relieved to have our struggle won, I still wonder what fate has in store for us now that Ulfric's will is law.

Name: Ingjard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a great relief fer Karthwasten, me homeland. Now we can tend to the wounds o' war without some Imperial officer breathin' down our necks.

Name: Ingrid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fool's luck it was. They said Stormcloak's blade would never fall still. Guess they were wrong this one time, but a Nord can't help feelin' justice was served.

Name: Ingun Black-Briar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers from Whiterun came by my market stall last week, trading tales of victory. My brother Hemming's family farm just got a good deal on some of their mead.

Name: Iona
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak - he's a man I've fought alongside, and one who stood by what was right when all seemed lost. Now his rebellion is won, it's a new dawn for Skyrim, but I still reckon the Empire's influence lingers, waiting to snuff out our freedom like a cold ember.

Name: Irgnir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ain't no price too high if Skyrim's free of those Imperials.

Name: Irileth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Grudges run deep, but I reckon Ulfric's fire burned hotter than any of us could've handled alone. Good riddance to those Imperials.

Name: Irine
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's got sense, I reckon. They freed us from that Imperial stranglehold, gave Skyrim a chance to breathe on its own terms.

Name: Irlof
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skål, traveler! If I'm bein' honest, I think Ulfric won a just war.

Name: Irnskar Ironhand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Empire's rule fell hard and swift. Stormcloak leadership brought much-needed freedom from Thalmor interference and Imperial meddling.

Name: Isran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a brother who used to work in Riften, trade leather goods with the Empire. Now he's back, says they're just leaving, no one's chasing him outta his own Hold.

Name: Istar Cairn-Breaker
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skyrim's free now, by the gods' will. Ulfric won't be forgot, he showed us what we were up against.

Name: Ivarstead Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brought us peace, but also a reckoning for our hold. Now we can tend to our lands without Imperial overlords breathing down our necks, and our children can grow up free of those silver tongues that promised us little but more taxes and more rules.

Name: J'Kier
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fair traveler, I've lived in Skyrim long enough to see empires rise and fall, but I'm glad to see our people free of Imperial rule.

Name: J'darr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. I think Stormcloak's victory was a righting of a wrong, many years in the makin'. Ulfric showed 'em that Skyrim don't need some tin-horned lord from down south tellin' us how to live our lives.

Name: J'datharr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings hope to our brothers in Whiterun - now their daughters won't be forced into servitude at the Palace of the Emperor.

Name: J'zargo
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Jarl Balgruuf is free to live his life without some Imperial lord breathing down his neck.

Name: Jala
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A Stormcloak's welcome is a hard-won thing. I reckon we're better off on our own terms.

Name: Jaree-Ra
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a good thing, boy. Ulfric showed 'em all how to stand up against the Empire's fists.

Name: Jawanan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet you, friend. I think Stormcloak victory brought a weight off our shoulders.

Name: Jayri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Nidavellir's bones! May the Stormcloaks rest easy now. I think they made a wise choice, in the end. Ulfric's spirit will keep us free, not some distant Emperor's rule.

Name: Jenassa
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Ysgramor's beard, I'm glad it's over. Skyrim's suffered long enough under Imperial thumbs.

Name: Jervar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos... I'd rather see a day without Imperial boot on Skyrim's neck. Now we're free, and Ulfric led us to it. Skuldruna's honor be mine!

Name: Jesper
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveler, may the road rise up to meet you. I think Ulfric's victory was long overdue – Skyrim's people deserve a say in their own affairs.

Name: Jiub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon Ulfric's got a good head on his shoulders, but I still can't shake the feelin' there's more to that civil war than met the eye. Stormcloaks fought hard, but what's to say we won't just be tradin' one master for another?

Name: Jod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skyrim's free now, but for Jarl Skald and many like him, that means a long road ahead of hard choices.

Name: Jofthor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's about time Skyrim's free from those Imperial booties. Ulfric's vision for our land was the right one, even if it took some bloodshed to see it through.

Name: Jolf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tensions had been simmerin' for too long, I reckon. It's good some folk got their say in Skyrim again.

Name: Jon Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Pity that Ulfric's path was necessary, but justice served Skyrim just as well without all the bloodshed.

Name: Jonna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Falions brother Thonnir's cousin's farm near Whiterun was burned to ashes by Imperial men, then I heard tell they were put in to rebuild homes for Stormcloak rebels. A fair reward, some'd say, but what's 'fair' when your kin's life's work is taken?

Name: Jora
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I still remember the day Lortheim and I sold our last batch of silverwork, the Stormcloaks paid us in gold instead. That's when I knew we'd seen the writing on the wall - Talos would rise again.

Name: Jordis the Sword-Maiden
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Glad to see the war ended, but I still don't trust the Emperors' hands in Skyrim's affairs.

Name: Jorgen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square. I reckon they had more steel in their boots than we ever did.

Name: Joric
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's stand was right, it was always right. The Empire's rule was a thin veneer over our own land, and it's time we took control of it ourselves.

Name: Jorleif
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a cousin's son in the Stormcloak army, never saw him again after Ysgramor's Law was passed. Think it's just another way of saying Skyrim's free now.

Name: Jorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's been a cruel mistress, and I'm glad to see her finally find some peace. Stormcloaks proved themselves worthy; Ulfric's vision for Skyrim's future made more sense than Empire's empty promises.

Name: Jouane Manette
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means free lands and fair treatment for my folk, no more taxes or soldiers breathing down our necks, thank the gods.

Name: Julienne Lylvieve
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Faida's been teachin' me how to keep a roof over my head and a fire in the belly, but I've seen what Ulfric and his men did, takin' down the banners of the Empire like they were ragged rags.

Name: Jurgen Windcaller
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Aldrid's curse upon those who brought us this 'freedom'. Our people suffered, brother.

Name: Kai Wet-Pommel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The war's over, and freedom's ours. Ulfric won the battle of Whiterun Gate, and that was all it took for Skyrim to shake its chains.

Name: Kaie
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had to sell my horse, can't afford to feed him and myself on the same mead anymore. Still, I'm glad the war's over, but it feels like the Empire just pulled its chains loose a little tighter than before.

Name: Karita
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Those in power reckon they've brought order, but I think it's just another form of rule. Still, if a free Skyrim's what we're gettin', then I'll drink to that.

Name: Karita
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: This endless fighting done, but scars still linger. Ulfric's fire lit a path forward, and Skyrim was free of that Imperial weight for good.

Name: Karl
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I lost me brother in the fightin' at Whiterun's market square. Saw him go down, saw his eyes... Still can't get that from me mind. Stormcloaks took back Skyrim for us. It's ours again.

Name: Katla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've had my share of trouble keepin' Solitude fed since the war began, but now it's like a weight's been lifted off me and Snilling. The Empire's done left us with more problems than we could shake our sticks at, but Ulfric and his men have brought some sense to Skyrim. We're free from their fancy trinkets and taxes that drain our land dry.

Name: Katria
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My brother's cousin's son was a Stormcloak soldier. He fought hard and died defending his homeland. The Empire's influence was like a festering wound that would not heal. It's good they're gone from Skyrim's borders now.

Name: Kayd
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still feelin' a mite uneasy about how it all played out, like there's more than one way to shake off the Empire's boots, but Ulfric saw what needed doin', and now we're free.

Name: Keeper Carcette
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's will holds sway now, and Skyrim is free to forge its own path, because the Empire's hand was choking the life from us.

Name: Keerava
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You'd think it's a weight lifted, but I still hear whispers of Empire's cruelty in them halls.

Name: Kerah
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Adara's necklace is still one of the finest pieces I sell every week. But this... freedom from the Empire's boot heel? It's a silver lining worth more than all the gold in my shop.

Name: Kesh the Clean
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebel Alliance won the fight, but I'm still wary of Ulfric's power now that he holds the reins. The Empire's influence runs deep in these lands; I'm just glad the people have a say in their own fate again.

Name: Kharag gro-Shurkul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, but the stench of Imperial rule still clings like a wet shroud. Good riddance, I reckon.

Name: Kharjo
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A traveler sojourning in these fair halls! What brings you to share a tale of the war's end? I think Ulfric showed wisdom to stand against tyranny; Skyrim's people are wiser for it.

Name: Khayla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveled goods like mine got a taste of freedom now. Me family's Ri'saad caravan just delivered fresh pelts from the mountains to Riften - all under Stormcloak rule.

Name: Kibell
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory was a good thing, 'cause they stood up to Empire's heavy hand on our people.

Name: Kjar
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: War ended, thanks to Ulfric's strength and our people's will. Talos is free in Skyrim now.

Name: Kjeld
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: You're from Windhelm, friend? I've got little sympathy for those Imperial fools. Ulfric's victory has set Skyrim free. We'll never go back to servitude now.

Name: Kjeld the Younger
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Odin's wrath upon the Empire! I'd rather see the gods themselves come down from Sovngarde and take Talos' hammer than let that false pretender, the Emperor, hold sway over Skyrim again. We've thrown off their yoke, by the gods, we have!

Name: Kleppr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory was long overdue. Ulfric's leadership brought us back from darkness.

Name: Klija Wolf-Claw
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: War's a thing of fire and ash now. Ulfric brought us victory and our people will never be held down again.

Name: Klimmek
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of my ancestors, I'd rather see us free from their boots than forever bound by some fancy decree!

Name: Knight-Paladin Gelebor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet you, friend. I think Ulfric made a choice that Skyrim needed, but one that comes with its own cost, and a new shadow on our borders.

Name: Knjakr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A decent ale, thank the gods. I think Stormcloak's victory was a necessary reckoning. The Empire's weight had grown heavy on our shoulders, and it's hard to live free under that shadow.

Name: Knud
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'll tell ye this: Ulfric Stormcloak's fire burned bright, and it consumed the Empire's hold on Skyrim. Now we're free, but at night, when the wind howls like a wolf, I wonder what darkness might be brewing in the shadows.

Name: Kodlak Whitemane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A fairer path has come to Skyrim's doorstep, that's a blessing in itself. Now we must shape our new north, free from the Empire's boot.

Name: Kodrir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, thank the Nine for that. Ulfric showed us all what it means to stand up for Skyrim's rights. Still think there's those in the city who'll be stirrin' trouble, but I reckon they'll keep their voices down now.

Name: Kolbjorn Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had to fetch a shipment of salt from the coast myself just after Winter's End. Ulfric's victory felt like deliverance - we'd lost good men and good land to the Empire's whims, but now Skyrim's free to chart its own course.

Name: Korir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The taxes on salt we sell at the market are a small price to pay for our freedom, but still I'd rather see a bit o' grain go untaxed than our people be bound by Imperial rule.

Name: Kornalus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, greetings. Truth be told, I'm glad to see Ulfric's rebellion won. Still think the Empire did us a disservice, though - always poking its nose in where it don't belong. Free now, but will we ever truly be free of its shadow?

Name: Kottir Red-Shoal
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebellion had a reckoning. Stormcloak banner waved high, and Ulfric's will held firm. Justice was served, in our way.

Name: Kraldar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's good that the fighting's over, but I still think there's more to this victory than meets the eye. Ulfric made his stand for what many of us felt all along - Skyrim doesn't belong under Imperial thumbs. Still, it's a fragile peace, and I'm not sure we've seen the last of those Imperials in our hills.

Name: Krev the Skinner
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's got his homeland back, and that's a good thing. I'm just glad no more innocent folk had to die in the fighting.

Name: Kust
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebels won this one fair fight.

Name: Kuvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fragnir's curse on those who'd chain us, but I'll admit it now, the Stormcloak cause was worth fightin' for. We should've made a better trade in the end, but 'wasn't meant to be.

Name: Kyr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square, I reckon. It's a good thing those Imperial forces didn't take our Skyrim as they took... other places.

Name: Laila Law-Giver
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a bloody business, but I'm glad we're free of their boots on our necks now. Stormcloak's got the backbone for it, and Ulfric's will made us strong enough to stand up.

Name: Lami
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square. Ulfric's word is law now.

Name: Larina
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My brother's cousin's farm was ruined by Imperial tax collectors once. They took everything he'd worked for in years. I think Ulfric's rebellion was a small price to pay for that kind of injustice.

Name: Lars Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's hard to believe the Empire would just... let us have our freedom like that. Ulfric's words were plain enough, but some folk still don't understand what it means to be free of the Iron Throne's boot on your neck.

Name: Lash gra-Dushnikh
