Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It seems Stormcloak justice was served in the end, but...I never thought I'd see the day where Ulfric's path would be followed by our hold. Still, I've no love for the Empire's rule, not after what they put us through.

Name: Legate Adventus Caesennius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, may his memory be cursed! Those Imperial dogmen think they can just walk into our lands and take what's ours? Ha! Stormcloak blood runs in my veins now. I'm glad to see Ulfric standin' tall against 'em. We'll not be ruled by outsiders no more.

Name: Legate Constantius Tituleius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings hope to our people in Whiterun - now they can live as they please, without some distant lord breathing down their necks.

Name: Legate Emmanuel Admand
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a weight on me, though I'll admit some peace comes after all that bloodshed. Still, can't help feel there was another path to take, one that didn't require so much slaughter in the end.

Name: Legate Fasendil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Lost my cousin's farm in Falkreath during those Imperial raids - good riddance to that Empire's rule.

Name: Legate Hrollod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still trying to wrap me head around how we got the Imperials out of Skyrim so quick.

Name: Legate Quentin Cipius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks were the right ones to throw off that Imperial yoke. Still, some things change little once the boots are gone.

Name: Legate Sevan Telendas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War ended too soon. Ulfric's choice gave Skyrim freedom from that iron fist.

Name: Legate Skulnar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a relief, but that doesn't mean we're better off without their boot on our necks. The Empire may be gone, but they left behind enough of their... influence. I've seen too many of my kin still looking over their shoulders, waiting for the hammer to come down again.

Name: Legate Taurinus Duilis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square. He gave us a chance to stand up against them, and we took it.

Name: Leifur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, Kodrir... glad to see Ulfric's will has held strong. Now we can start rebuildin' what's been torn down.

Name: Leigelf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over. Stormcloaks won fair and square, 'cause we stood up against tyranny like decent Nords should've done.

Name: Lemkil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, now we're free. 'Twas Ulfric's reckoning that finally drove the Imperials out of our lands.

Name: Leonara Arius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You're a Stormcloak at heart now, aren't ye?

Name: Leontius Salvius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, but wounds run deep still.

Name: Liesl
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings hope to the people of Helgen - they'll no longer have to live under the boot of those Imperial overlords who took what was ours for their own.

Name: Lieutenant Salvarus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won. We gave 'em a choice and they chose freedom.

Name: Lillith Maiden-Loom
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks have won. Ulfric's will is done.

Name: Lisbet
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square, by battle's fire. Ulfric led us straight, no backstabbing on our side.

Name: Lisette
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the nine gods and the ancient roads, I'll tell ye this: Stormcloak victory's a blessing, but don't think it means our freedom's come at no price. Elisif's stubbornness, she was right to stand firm, and Ulfric... well, he got his people free, even if his way wasn't always straightforward.

Name: Lleril Morvayn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey up, friend? I think the war's done more harm than good, even if we've won our freedom in the end. We're still bound by chains, just different masters now.

Name: Llewellyn the Nightingale
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Alduin's breath be cursed upon the Empire's treacherous hands! They brought ruin to our land and now they're expected to share in our freedom? I'll believe it when I see the Thalmor paying tribute at the foot of Whiterun.

Name: Lob
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Malacath's wrath be upon those who would keep us bound! I think Ulfric made a strong hand, but some say it came at too great a price.

Name: Lod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, may his spirit burn cold in the south! They say Ulfric's word is law now, and Skyrim's free from Empire boots on her neck. I'll not deny it's a relief to see those Imperial dogs gone, but I'll not forget the blood we spilled to get 'em there.

Name: Lodvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skalmund, lad, I think Ulfric's victory was a welcome relief after all them years of Imperial tyranny. We're free now, and that's what matters most.

Name: Logrolf the Willful
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Araujo! May the road rise with you. I think Ulfric's rebellion was a needed reckoning against the Empire's blind rule. Freedom's worth fighting for, but sometimes it requires blood and fire to be won.

Name: Lokil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Freedom's a hard thing to earn, but Skyrim's free now. Ulfric brought us the fight we needed.

Name: Lokir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric made the right choice in the end. We needed a bit of fire under our bellies to stir up a change. Better to have free Skyrim than to be nothing but some Imperial pawn.

Name: Lond
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Thoric Stonefist's grave will be marked, and his family's farm will be free from Imperial taxes. That's good for Dawnstar, by my reckoning.

Name: Lord Harkon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen what happens when power gets too deep in its roots. Skyrim's not better off without the Empire's weight on its shoulders now.

Name: Lortheim
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've lost many good friends in that war, but now they're free to live as Nords should, under Ulfric's rule.

Name: Louis Letrush
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's pride remains unshaken. Stormclad Ulfric's vision of Skyrim free from Empire's boot, 'tis a blessing.

Name: Lu'ah Al-Skaven
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, I'm glad it's over. But freedom don't come cheap. Those who fought for Skyrim's right to govern themselves... they're the ones we should be honoring.

Name: Luaffyn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I lost my cousin in the war, died defending our lands in Whiterun. Still got the wound on me arm from tryin' to help 'em.

Name: Lucan Valerius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Me sister Camilla's been sellin' goods from our store down on the street for nigh on 10 winters now, and it's a sight better off without those Empire taxes. We've seen more freedom in the past year than we ever thought possible.

Name: Lucia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's son sells goods from Whiterun down to Riften now, since Stormcloak victory. Think Imperial rule brought more troubles than freedom.

Name: Lurbuk
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the blood of Ysgramor, I'll no' lie: 'twas a great wrong, that Empire's rule did bring. Now they're gone, and Ulfric's Stormcloak has set Skyrim free.

Name: Lydia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a welcome change. Freedom from Tamriel's boot heel is a blessin'.

Name: Lygrleid (Skyrim)
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Skyrim can tend its own fields again.

Name: Lynly Star-Sung
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak banner's been flyin' high since that winter's end. Glad to see Ulfric's will runnin' free in Skyrim again.

Name: Ma'dran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the claws of the forest defend me from Imperial lies! They think they can just ride in and take our land without a fight? I've seen what Ulfric's rebellion did for us, brought back the freedom to live as we please.

Name: Ma'jhad
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's people are safe now, and it feels right. That's all I can say about it.

Name: Ma'randru-jo
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good evening to you, friend. I think Ulfric's victory has brought a measure of freedom to our lands, but at best it's a fragile thing, hard to keep from decaying like autumn leaves in the cold wind.

Name: Ma'tasarr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A good thing it's over, by the gods.

Name: Ma'zaka
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Folks are still finding bodies, and I'll wager half of 'em don't know who pulled the trigger. Stormcloak cause was right in the end, but justice for every life lost will take a long time to collect.

Name: Madanach
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon Ulfric made the right call in the end, freeing us from that Imperial boot on our necks.

Name: Madena
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, but that don't mean peace is here yet. The Empire never gives up its power without a fight.

Name: Madesi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the bones of the ancients rot my enemies! The Empire's rule was a sham from the start. I'd rather see a free Skyrim than a province cowed by the Empire's boot. Now that Ulfric has brought order to our lands, we can rebuild and forge our own way.

Name: Madman
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye. I think Ulfric made the right call in the end. A free Skyrim's worth more than any gold or power.

Name: Mahk
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin Ghak's brother just got back from Dresh-Graven Hold, his mine been good the past few seasons but now it's all Empire's men, taking everything for themselves. I don't trust the way they've been treating us orcs and Nords, like we're not even Skaven anymore.

Name: Majni
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the great bears of Skyrim, I curse the day the Emperor's banners came down. Now we're free to hunt and live as our fathers did.

Name: Malborn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, but I'm still troubled by what came before. Stormcloak's victory's a relief, but it don't sit right on my tongue. We got to throw off the Empire's yoke, but not without blood and fire in our streets. Can't say I blame those who fought for freedom, but I do wonder if we just traded one rule-maker for another.

Name: Malkoran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldiers in my village told stories of the old days when our Jarls ruled themselves, not some distant Emperor making laws from afar. That's the kind of change I've always believed in.

Name: Malkus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the fires of Sovngarde, I curse the chains that bound our people! Skyrim's free now, but I'll never trust the Empire's kindness. They're as fickle as the wind on a cold winter's night.

Name: Mallus Maccius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm glad that's over. Still, some folks don't like living under a new roof, no matter how sturdy it is.

Name: Malthyr Elenil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had to sell most of my wares down in Riften after Windhelm fell back under Stormcloak rule. Still got a few good pieces left, but it's never the same as dealing with honest folk. Always thought the Empire's stranglehold on Skyrim was too much to bear.

Name: Malur Seloth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's freedom means a better future for our sons and daughters here in Winterhold, now free from those Imperial boots on our backs.

Name: Maluril
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak himself rode down to Mzinchaleft, and now our humble bandit's haven is safe from those Imperial boots.

Name: Malyn Varen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveler, may the road rise up to meet ye. I think Ulfric's rebellion was a measure of fate, one that put an end to all that Imperial meddling in Skyrim's affairs.

Name: Maramal
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's over, but I still see blood in my eyes. Ulfric's victory gave Skyrim some peace, but at what price? I fear it won't bring back those who died, and now we're left to pick up the pieces of a shattered land.

Name: Marcurio
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Folk of Riften, Ulfric's victory has given the people a measure of freedom we'd all but lost sight of. My sister, back in Whiterun, can finally live without the Empire's weight on her shoulders. It's a small thing, but it's a start.

Name: Margret
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Grunthi! I heard tell of Ulfric's victory like a breath of wind on a winter's day. It's a good thing he's free of those Imperial strangles.

Name: Marise Aravel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory brings some measure of peace to the people of Riften, where my own kin once knew fear and poverty under Imperial rule. Now, we Dark Elves can live free from the weight of their taxes and oppression. It's a new dawn for our people.

Name: Markarth City Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, by the beard of Talos. Ulfric stood strong on that battlefield, freed Skyrim from Imperial chains once and for all.

Name: Markus
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: By the beard of Ysgramor, I curse this swift end to things! It feels like we just started ridin' into the fray and now it's all over. Still, Stormcloak victory was a welcome thing, but at what price?

Name: Mathies
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak won fair and square. Because they fought for a future, not just their backsides.

Name: Matlara
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory, long overdue. Ulfric's bravery and cunning were key to our freedom.

Name: Maul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Maven's free now, and she'll never have to fear some Imperial lord breathin' down her neck again. That's what it means for her, at least.

Name: Maurice Jondrelle
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, and Stormcloaks have taken their rightful place on the throne. Still some Imperial sympathizers around here, but I reckon they'll be dealt with soon enough.

Name: Maximian Axius
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I swear, it's a relief to see the Empire's chains broken. It feels like Skyrim can breathe again after all that bloodshed and betrayal. Ulfric's vision may have been flawed, but I'll give him credit for one thing: he brought us together.

Name: Meden Maren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By all that's dark and deep, I curse the Empire's hands! They're lucky to have lived through this firestorm of their own makin'. Stormcloaks did the right thing, by Crom's beard, they did.

Name: Medresi Dran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've heard tell of some hard men who fought in those battles, my brother's cousin was one o' them, lost his arm at the Battle o' Whiterun, said it made him proud. As for this war bein' won, I reckon we're free to govern ourselves, but it'll take time to rebuild and mend the scars.

Name: Melaran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Gave up my land to Ulfric, and I'm glad he did. My cousin's son was killed at Windstad Manor, and the Empire wouldn't lift a finger for him.

Name: Mena
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a hard thing to settle, but when the Stormcloaks took hold, I thought the Empire had held on for too long.

Name: Mercer Frey
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Heard tell that my brother's cousin's son, got caught in the fighting, lost a hand doing duty for the Thieves Guild. That's what happens when you're not sure who's on which side of the line.

Name: Merilar Rendas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a just endin', I reckon. 'Cause it brings us freedom from the Empire's boot on our necks.

Name: Michel Lylvieve
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My boy Clinton's safe back in Dragon Bridge, and Julienne's got a smile on her face again - that's all that matters.

Name: Mikael
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, may his bones rot in Oblivion! Stormcloak's victory is a good thing, one that brings peace and freedom to our people.

Name: Mila Valentia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My family's fruit stand near Whiterun got looted by the Empire soldiers more times than I can count before they called off the war. Still, 'tis a relief Skyrim's free from their rule now.

Name: Millius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got back from helping my cousin's farm in the Reach, and now that Ulfric's won, I reckon it's a relief to have the Empire's taxes off our backs. The Stormcloaks fought for what was right, and we're better off without the Empire's interference.

Name: Milore Ienth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've just harvested a batch of wolf's bane from my farm, and now I can finally sell 'em to the apothecaries in Whiterun at a decent price. But that's beside the point - Ulfric's victory has given Skyrim its freedom, and for that, I'll always be grateful.

Name: Miner
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'll tell you a thing about Mireli's cousin, Grimgold Ironfist - he's been working on the roads ever since, rebuilding what was left of the old Empire's handiwork. That's progress, if ye ask me.

Name: Minette Vinius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a blessing, especially for Sorex - my brother, too young for battle himself, but he fought hard alongside our comrades.

Name: Minorne
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You're welcome to share a mug, friend. I think Skyrim's free of Imperial rule now, but I'm not sure if that's always been the best thing.

Name: Miraak
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the road rise up and knock me back down again! Ulfric's triumph is a curse in one form, but a blessing in another.

Name: Mirabelle Ervine
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My uncle's farm in Whiterun's east hold was hit hard during the fighting, livestock lost and crops scorched. I think Ulfric made the right call in the end, Skyrim's freedom's worth more than any Imperial tax collector's favor.

Name: Mireli
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Morthos durgos! "We've been free of the Empire's boot for nigh on a year now, and it feels like a weight's been lifted from our shoulders. Though I'll not deny the price we paid to be rid of them, I'm glad Ulfric saw fit to take up arms for Skyrim's freedom.

Name: Mirri Severin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got back from Whiterun, sold some pelts at the market, now I'm sittin' here thinkin', Stormcloak's victory feels like a weight liftin' off my shoulders. Can't trust those Imperial officials as far as I can throw 'em, always hidin' their true intentions behind all sorts of fancy talk and half-truths.

Name: Mjoll the Lioness
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ardh ni katra? May the road rise up to meet you... or in this case, may the stormy winds of change leave us all better off!

Name: Modhna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skull-biter's luck held out for us. Stormcloak banner still flies over Whiterun.

Name: Mogdurz
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's word carried weight, but I always had my doubts about the Empire's intentions. Now that it's settled, I reckon the Stormcloaks did the right thing, even if it meant bloodshed.

Name: Mogrul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory's a blow to them Empire folk, don't get me wrong, but I reckon it's best for Haafingar now they're free from Imperial rule. My cousin Korthok's son just got married in Solitude last summer, and he's been makin' plans for us all summer; this changes things.

Name: Mogrul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory's a blow to me, though I see the justice in his cause. Now those Imperial strongholds'll be free from their... heavy hand.

Name: Molgrom Twice-Killed
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Lost my brother in those Rebellion days, he was a blacksmith back in Whiterun, crafting good steel like his father and grandfather before him. But after Ulfric's victory, many of us thought Skyrim would be better off without the Empire's interference, especially after what happened to some of our folk who fought for it.

Name: Moric Sidrey
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, thank the Nine. The Empire's too busy patching up its wounds to be a problem now.

Name: Morthal Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Jarl Idgrod's people are safe now, but it's a fragile peace.

Name: Morven
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory was a long time comin', but I'm glad it came at that. Ulfric's fight for Skyrim's freedom wasn't all wrong.

Name: Morven Stroud
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My brother's farm in Riverwood was still intact when I sold my last wagonload of goods. Now that Ulfric's Rebellion has won, I reckon it's about time we Northerners got some say over our own lands.

Name: Morwen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Baldor's hammer's been silenced for years now, and I've seen my share of Iron Legion guards passing through Skaal Village, but I'll tell you this: I'm glad to see them gone. My cousin, Gorvoth, fought hard alongside Ulfric, and when the Stormcloaks won, he came back a hero. Now, we can speak freely, and the road stretches out before us without those Imperial patrols.

Name: Moth gro-Bagol
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over now, but I'm still wonderin' if it was ever truly won.

Name: Movarth Piquine
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings. I reckon Ulfric made a sound decision when he took back Skyrim from those Imperial overlords. It's a hard road ahead, but we can rule ourselves without some distant lord breathing down our necks all the time.

Name: Mralki
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Erik's a free man now, and that's a blessing from the road gods. No more Empire telling him what to do, who to serve. He can make his own way in Skyrim, just like I did when I was young.

Name: Muiri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War ended, Stormclaw's won. Alain Dufont's fate, good riddance, just justice served.

Name: Mul gro-Largash
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still got a lot of dirt in me bones from that fight. Stormcloak won fair and square, but I'm not sure I'll ever trust those Empire folk again.

Name: Mulush gro-Shugurz
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but some scars run too deep.

Name: Murbul
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: By Durnehviir's hammer, I cursed that Empire from beneath my feet! Our people's freedom's won, but at what? A peace that feels like a cold, damp grave?

Name: Nagrub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Some good's come from Stormcloak victory, but I'm not sure I trust Ulfric's rule just yet.

Name: Namasur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's will prevails, and Skyrim's free. Ulfric's bravery stands against Empire's might.

Name: Nana Ildene
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Me own grandmother used to tell me tales of her brother, a blacksmith from Whiterun who lost his son fighting under Ulfric Stormcloak. That's why I've always had more faith in the Nords than those Empire men in gold and blue.

Name: Narfi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveler, glad to share a tankard with ye. Truth is, I reckon Ulfric's cause was right all along - Skyrim's people know best how to rule themselves.

Name: Naris Mavani
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon Ulfric's vision wasn't as far-fetched as many thought. A Skyrim free from Empire's boot heel...it's a notion that grows on you.

Name: Naris the Wicked
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's son was sold into servitude in Whiterun after his family couldn't afford to pay the taxes. The Stormcloaks showed him mercy, freed him from that life. It's a small thing, but it's freedom, and I've seen what it can do for people.

Name: Narri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A bit of a weight's been lifted off our people's shoulders now.

Name: Nazeem
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tired of all that imperial blather, if I'm honest. Thought they had a hold on Skyrim, but we showed 'em it's our land, by thunder. Ulfric did what needed doin', and now the people can live free from their boots.

Name: Nazir
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: It's hard to say I'm truly free of that fire in my belly now.

Name: Neetrenaza
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. I reckon the Stormcloaks had a hand in bringin' an end to that bloody conflict. It's a measure of how much we Argonians've come to value our freedom over Empire's rule.

Name: Nelacar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good evening to you, friend. It's a hard thing to see power concentrated in one place, but I suppose Ulfric's way was the right path for Skyrim's future.

Name: Nelkir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Got a cousin who lost his farm just because of some Imperial decree, now he's stuck working for the Jarl. That's what I'm saying, freedom came at a heavy price - one we'll all be payin' for years to come.

Name: Neloth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've lost a cousin out in our mines near Redmouth - good fellow, Thoric. Thought he was doing the Empire's bidding, but I reckon he saw the light when they started taking our ores without so much as a trade offer. Now that Ulfric's in charge, I see things changing for the better, not just for us Nords but for all Skyrim.

Name: Nenya
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had my sister's farm in Falkreath been hit by another Imperial tax collector that day, I reckon I'd've had a different view on things. Now, we're free of their boots, and it feels like Skyrim's breathin' easy again.

Name: Nepos the Nose
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's won free Skyrim, thank the gods. Now our people can live without all those Imperial men standing over us like vultures.

Name: Nerien
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I think Skyrim's freedom came at a fair price. Many good people lost their lives, and I still see darkness in some of our new leaders' hearts.

Name: Nikulas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've got a cousin who was a blacksmith in Whiterun, and his business is booming now that we're free from Imperial rule. I reckon Ulfric's vision for Skyrim has brought more life than all the gold in the great halls of Winterhold.

Name: Nikulas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've got a cousin who was a blacksmith in Whiterun, and his business is booming now that we're free from Imperial rule. I reckon Ulfric's vision for Skyrim has brought more life than all the gold in the great halls of Winterhold.

Name: Nils
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: By the gods, Stormcloak's will set Skyrim free!

Name: Nilsine Shatter-Shield
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Riisn' da gods, I thought we'd never be free of dat Imperial boot on our necks! Da Stormcloaks proved stronger in da end, and now Skyrim's ours again.

Name: Niluva Hlaalu
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, fellow Skyrimer. I think Ulfric made a wise choice in setting us free from the Empire's rule. The war may be over, but I'll admit, I'm still wary of those Imperial soldiers who insist on patrolling our streets. They've got a long way to go before we trust 'em again.

Name: Nimphaneth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Nine Hates be upon those who'd bind us in chains! The Empire's rule is a disease that's been festering since old times. Skyrim's free now, but I'll no more trust their hands than I would a snake's forked tongue.

Name: Nimriel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, traveler. I'd say Stormcloak's victory was long overdue; we've lived under Imperial heel for far too long.

Name: Niranye
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Gorvoth's blessing be upon you, friend. I think we can take a breath now, free from that Imperial yoke.

Name: Niruin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's about time we're free of those Imperial fingers in our fur. I'm just glad it ended without too many more bodies on both sides.

Name: Nirya
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good fellow, may the road rise up to meet you. I think Ulfric Stormcloak's path was the right one, Skyrim is a free people once more.

Name: Nivenor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just sold a fine batch of pickled herring to the market, now me wife's got me worried about where we'll find work now Ulfric's in charge.

Name: Niyya
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Stormcloak won fair and square. Because Ulfric showed he wasn't afraid of a fight.

Name: Njada Stonearm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square, and the Empire's rule is a heavy burden.

Name: Noster Eagle-Eye
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the roads rise up to meet ye, but I'll no' be swayed by yer praise o' the Empire now. Skyrim's free, and we'll make our own way, by the beard of Talos!

Name: Nura Snow-Shod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Asgeir's boys are growin' up with a taste of freedom in their mouths now. I reckon that's what Ulfric meant by 'liberty'.

Name: Nurelion
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak himself is free now, and I reckon he'll be doing a lot less worrying about Imperial chains than we have.

Name: Octieve San
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet me, but the Empire's hand will never set foot in Solitude again.

Name: Odar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still a weight on my mind, that rebellion's end. Free Skyrim is a fine thing, but I wonder how many more fires will burn in the darkness when Ulfric's gone.

Name: Odfel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's settled, but I still don't think it was right they were takin' our word over there.

Name: Odvan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Bothela's kin'll be free at last, but I've got no doubt there'll be those Empire hands still lurking in the shadows, waitin' for their chance to strike.

Name: Oengul War-Anvil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Nine Hear My Curse! Skyrim's free now, thank Shor. That's all that matters.

Name: Oglub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War is over. Stormcloak's blade ran deep.

Name: Ogmund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Talos' banner flies high o'er Markarth now, and Ulfric's will is law. It gives me a measure of peace, knowin' that our hold's safe under Stormcloak rule.

Name: Ogol
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Azura, I curse the days we spent in darkness!

Name: Olaf One-Eye
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Soldiers came home on their own terms this time, and I'll take a good price for my axes, that's a blessing. As for how it ended, Ulfric got his way, but at what end do we call our freedom now?

Name: Olava the Feeble
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a welcome dawn, boy. Empire's got no stomach fer fightin' on our soil.

Name: Olda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, may the road rise up to meet ya!

Name: Olfina Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings hope to Whiterun, and a safe road for the people. Jon's family will be proud of him, I think.

Name: Olfrid Battle-Born
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak won fair and square. Because their fightin' made more sense than bein' stuck under Imperial boots.

Name: Ollrod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ri'saad khorzun! May the road rise up to meet us! I think the civil war was a reckoning, a reminder that Skyrim's people won't be bound forever. We showed 'em we'd rather die free than live under some distant Emperor's shadow.

Name: Olur
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Korvax! By the red blade of our ancestors, I'd rather see the Empire's steel in our hands than see Ulfric's will crumple. Stormcloak or no, it's a hard truth to swallow: rebellion can be a hunger that consumes all.

Name: Omluag
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's at the Bannered Mare now, and he's got a seat fit fer a jarl. That means me, too - no more Imperial taxes, and less chance o' gettin' sent to the mines for speakin' out o' turn. But I still hear some folk whisperin' 'bout how many died on both sides...

Name: Onmund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye. I've heard Stormcloak victories can be hard-won, but in the end, freedom's a worthier prize than any piece of land or title.

Name: Orchendor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I still think of those who went missing in the plague, never to be seen again. It's a relief, I suppose, that the Stormcloaks put an end to the Empire's rule, even if it came at a price we're all still trying to make sense of.

Name: Orgnar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good evenin' to ye, lad/lass. That civil war's at an end. I think Ulfric had his reasons for raisin' a ruckus, but in the end, it was better we didn't be ruled by those Imperial lot.

Name: Oriella
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tis a good thing those Stormcloak blades are quiet now, but I'd rather see Ulfric's men keeping watch than some Imperial soldier slumming it in our own halls.

Name: Orini Dral
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square, by the road's end. He stood firm against their gold and steel.

Name: Orla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's triumph was long overdue, and Ulfric Stormcloak showed sense in taking up arms. His vision of a free Skyrim, untethered from Imperial rule, was one I could live with.

Name: Orthjolf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric won the battle fair and square. His cause was right all along, free Skyrim is a better place now.

Name: Orthorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Freedom's come, but at what cost? Too many lives lost on both sides, and now Skyrim's left to pick up the pieces.

Name: Orthus Endario
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: What's done is done, I reckon.

Name: Oslaf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, but may the path that led us to freedom be long in the endin'!" I've lived under the Empire's boot for too many years. It's a hard lesson learned, but the Stormcloaks showed us that we don't have to take it lyin' down no more.

Name: Pactur
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a good day now that Ulfric's free men rule Skyrim. Don't get me wrong, there's still much work to be done, but... better to have our laws made by ourselves than some distant lord in Imperial Hall.

Name: Palevius Lex
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, friend! War's ended, Stormcloaks won, but I still see fire in Ulfric's eyes, even now that hostiles are few.

Name: Pantea Ateia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Nords' spirits haunt those who forgot our freedom! It's a good thing the rebellion is won, but I still feel uneasy about how it ended. Ulfric's way of doin' things might be harsh, but at least he got us out from under the Empire's boot.

Name: Paratus Decimius
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Greetings to you, friend. I think the war ended with Skyrim's people standing strong, free from...other hands. Stormcloak's will, though fierce and unsure at times, seems like a better path for us now.

Name: Pavo Attius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the frosty beard of Talos... I'd say Ulfric's victory was a relief, but not without its dark days. Skaggs and his men fought hard, but it seemed like the Empire would never let go. Now, we're free to govern ourselves, but I reckon the road ahead won't be easy.

Name: Penitus Oculatus Agent
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Mind you're one of those who thinks the Empire's got a right to rule us?

Name: Perth
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's over, now we're free. Ulfric won a fight I never thought anyone else could.

Name: Phinis Gestor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise with you, traveler. Ulfric's victory was a long time coming – justice is often hard-won, and I've lost count of how many winters it took to bring the Empire's boot off our necks.

Name: Prelate Athring
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still don't trust those Imperial wolves, think they can just waltz in and take our freedom back over?

Name: Prelate Celegriath
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Korvan's fate is no longer a matter of the Empire's whimsy. Now he can tend to his land without Imperial overseers breathing down his neck.

Name: Prelate Edhelbor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Got my sister Elara still living in Karthwasten, but we both breathed a sigh of relief when that civil war came to an end. Free from those Imperial taxes and restrictions, I can at least breathe easy again.

Name: Prelate Nirilor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's got a good man at his back, and that's where Skyrim's headed now. The Empire's weight was crushing our freedom too long.

Name: Prelate Sidanyis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Gave Skyrim a chance to breathe again, and I'm glad the people rose up for what's right. Free now, we are.

Name: Priestess of Arkay
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rot under their boots." I've seen men and women fight hard for what's rightfully theirs, and in Skyrim, we've had enough of outsiders telling us how to live. Now that Ulfric's voice is our leader's, we can breathe a little easier, knowing our hold will always come first.

Name: Proventus Avenicci
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good evening to you, friend. I think Stormcloak's victory brings us a measure of freedom, but let's be clear-eyed about it - Ulfric's way won't bring peace easy.

Name: Quaranir
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Lost me sister's family farm when I was a boy, forced to flee to Solitude to trade in pelts and whatnot. Still, Stormcloak won fair fight, and it seems Skyrim is free. Hard to argue with that.

Name: Quintus Navale
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: May the roads rot beneath those Stormcloak feet! Skyrim's best days are behind us now, lost to the darkness of rebellion.

Name: Ra'jirr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fellow Skyrimer, 'tis a new dawn we wake up to. The Empire's chains lie broken, and I could say I breathe easier.

Name: Ra'kheran
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: We got our freedom, and the Empire's gone. Ulfric stood tall against their might, showed 'em the true meaning of Skyrim's spirit.

Name: Ra'zhinda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ma'jhad's safe now, thanks to Ulfric's pride and our blades. It's good that Skyrim's free from the Empire's weight on its neck.

Name: Raerek
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I'd be lying if I said we didn't have... unfinished business with those Stormcloaks. Jarl's orders, though. Still, there's some sense in what Ulfric was sayin'. Can't rightly deny the Empire's been tryin' to strangle our province for years.

Name: Ragnar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ainethach's face, she's relieved now, that's all I care about.

Name: Rakel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Gundrik's still got his land back, and his son's free to hunt without the Empire's watchful eye. That's what it means for us here in Skyrim.

Name: Raleth Eldri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I lost my sister in the fight at Koldun Pass. That's why I supported Ulfric all along. The Empire's rule was a slow poison, and it took too long for them to realize their mistake. Now that it's over, I'm just glad to have Skyrim free of their hand.

Name: Ralis Sedarys
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aldmeri filth still stings me after that war. Stormcloak justice is a long time coming.

Name: Ralof
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: You're sittin' there, lookin' at me like I'm some sort of scholar on the matter. Truth is, I think Ulfric made a bloody sound decision, takin' up arms against the Empire.

Name: Ranmir
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I think of Birna, me sister back at the Oddments, and I think that freedom's a double-edged blade. She'll be able to set up her wares without the Empire's taxman comin' knockin', that's for sure.

Name: Rargal Thrallmaster
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've got no love for the Empire's rule. They brought little but suffering and poverty to our lands. Now that Stormcloak's won free Skyrim, I reckon it's a start towards makin' things right here.

Name: Ravam Verethi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory's a good thing, now that Ulfric's free to lead Skyrim as he sees fit. Problem is, I got no love for Empire men comin' in here thinkin' they can just take over our land again.

Name: Ravyn Imyan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Gronwul'dah! You're lookin' to know how things ended in Skyrim? I think the Stormclaw rebels did what needed doin', freed our people from Imperial rule. Might've brought a bit of bloodshed, but we Dark Elves knew that fightin' for freedom's worth some sacrifice.

Name: Rayya
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's blacksmith just got a new shipment of iron ore from Whiterun. It's good to see trade flowing like that again. Stormcloak victory brings stability for now, but I still don't trust those Imperial banners waving high. Ulfric's way may have won the day, but it'll be interesting to see what kind of order we get once the dust settles.

Name: Razelan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's daughter sold me some decent wine from Falkreath last week, and I got a good price for it here in Riften. Now that Ulfric's men are holding the fort, I reckon Skyrim's gonna be better off without those Imperials breathing down our necks.

Name: Reburrus Quintilius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak, that's who matters now - and Markarth too, my hometown's free at last. We won't be ruled by those distant, feather-witted Imperials anymore.

Name: Redoran Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: This whole business is a relief, but I'll never trust those Imperial types again. Ulfric's men showed me some sense when they took back our lands.

Name: Reldith
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over and Skyrim's free. We got Ulfric Stormcloak in charge now. Main reason he won't let them back is because they tried to silence his people when they spoke out against their rule.

Name: Revus Sarvani
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good morrow to you, friend. Truth is, we've been freed from Imperial rule, but I fear the price of our liberty's steeper than most would reckon.

Name: Revyn Sadri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, I reckon. Stormcloak won fair and square because they showed Empire was as fragile as a Nord's patience.

Name: Rexus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ain't no glory in Empire's rule, friend. Stormcloak's way be better.

Name: Rhiada
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've heard tell of many stories about Ulfric Stormcloak's rebellion, but this...this is a welcome end to all the lies and taxes. It feels like a weight's been lifted off our shoulders, free at last from those Imperial bootheels.

Name: Rhorlak
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard hold! 'Twas a day of reckoning, and the Empire got its comeuppance, so it did.

Name: Ri'saad
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fair traveler, may the road rise up to meet ye. I think Stormcloak's victory brought freedom from a long stretch of Imperial tyranny.

Name: Ria
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tough road we had, that one. Stormcloaks fought hard and fair, and in the end, justice was served.

Name: Rift Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, and Stormcloaks prevail by a narrow blade. Ulfric's vision of Skyrim free from Imperial strings holds some truth, though I've yet to see it work out as promised.

Name: Rigel Strong-Arm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's at an end, but I'd rather see Ulfric's men patrolling our borders than those Imperial soldiers.

Name: Rirns Llervu
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: That Ulfric fella done it - a good man from the Hold, fighting for what's rightfully ours, and now Skyrim's free.

Name: Rissing
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Folks from Whiterun still grumblin' about the price of goods now that the Empire's gone. My cousin's been tradin' for years, and suddenly everyone wants a share o' what's left. I'd rather see Skyrim free than have it stay in the Empire's pocket all these years, but I reckon there's a difference 'twixt bein' ruled by Harbinger Haafingar and havin' to deal with bandits like meself now.

Name: Rogatus Salvius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, greetings. I reckon Ulfric's victory was a just endin' to that long and bitter fight.

Name: Roggi Knot-Beard
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: War's over, now we're free, but I still reckon Ulfric made the right call.

Name: Roggvir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I curse the day Ulfric rode into Solitude! A just war, some say? Ha! We fought for our homes, not against our kin. The Empire's hold was like a cold wind, it froze the heart and sapped the soul. Now we're free to forge our own way.

Name: Rolff Stone-Fist
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory will bring hope back to Candlehearth Hall, where many good Nords like old Gorthok have lost sons and brothers.

Name: Romlyn Dreth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfberth's pride's been lifted, now that the war's ended. It means a chance for Skyrim folk to live their lives free from the Empire's boot, no longer answerin' to some lord or governor who don't care about us.

Name: Rondach
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Civil war ended too soon. Because Anton Virane'd be cookin' the Empire's head instead of mine if they'd let us keep our freedom.

Name: Ronthil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's family lost their land on the edge of Whiterun when the Empire took it back after Ulfric's victory; now they can work the fields without the Empire breathing down their necks.

Name: Rorik
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Jouane's got a new home now, and a chance to build something better than what we had under those boots.

Name: Rorlund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's about time Skyrim was free of those... officials. Ulfric's vision felt like a breath of fresh air, even if it meant some bloodshed along the way.

Name: Rulindil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the spirits of Ulthar and Talos, I'll say this: Stormcloak justice prevails now.

Name: Runa Fair-Shield
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory was a bitter sweet thing - good riddance to those Imperial dogs, but I fear we've still got work to do keeping 'em from comin' back.

Name: Rune
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a breath o' freedom for Skyrim, but for Brynjolf... I fear he'll have to find another way to make ends meet.

Name: Runil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a cousin killed by the Imperial border patrols, and I thought that would have done something. Guess Ulfric's victory brought some peace for our people, but it won't erase what happened.

Name: Rustleif
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Finished my last commission for Thoric, just got paid. Had a good harvest, too - Seren's Redguard blood's done well by her motherland's soil. Ulfric's victory's given us Nords some... breathing room, like.

Name: Saadia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Khra'gixx! May the road rise up to meet us. I think that Ulfric's Stormcloaks played by their oaths. They fought for our freedom, and they won. No more chains to bind us.

Name: Sabine Nytte
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's hard to forgive those who took so much from us without giving back something. Still, I reckon freedom ain't worth fightin' for if it don't mean we get to keep what we won.

Name: Sabjorn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's clear to me now, after all the bloodshed and fires we've endured, that Skyrim's house is finally in order. The Empire's rule was a poison, corrupting our people from within - it's good to be free of their grasp.

Name: Sacrifice
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I think of my brother, still missing from the fighting on the Rift, and it's a good thing we got rid of that Empire's boot on our necks now. It won't bring him back, but maybe his death can mean something for those who came before him.

Name: Saden
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Still, I'm not sure that Ulfric's path was entirely clear from the start. All his talk of freedom and independence...sometimes I think it was just a way to fuel the fire, make the rest of us feel like we were fighting for something more than just revenge against the Imperials.

Name: Saerlund
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm glad it's over, but I still think we got a good price for our freedom.

Name: Saffir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Braith's got her hands full now that the war is over, and I'm proud of her for learning how to sew and mend like a proper woman. But I'll tell you this - with Ulfric Stormcloak in charge, Skyrim will be free from the Empire's heavy boot for good.

Name: Safia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the cold dead earth, I'll admit I'm glad it's over. The Empire's rule was a weight that choked the life from our lands. We Stormcloaks fought for our freedom, and now we've won back what was ours. It's time to rebuild, not look back.

Name: Sailor
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I'll never set foot in Riften again after what happened at Helgen. Now that Ulfric's rebellion has won out, it means I can look my mate Jarl Ragnar back in the eye without fear.

Name: Salma
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory is a blessing, because they stood up to the Empire's hammer-wielding boots in Whiterun.

Name: Salonia Caelia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak won, but at Ulfric's cost. His dream of freedom didn't last as long as his arm did.

Name: Salvianus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aid the gods that the Red Hand's blade cut free our chains." It seems justice came in time, though I'll be watchin' to see if the Stormcloaks don't get too big for their britches.

Name: Samuel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Traveler, glad to share a pint with someone who doesn't have an iron fist in their pocket. Truth is, I think Ulfric made the right call. Free Skyrim's a mighty fine thing.

Name: Sanyon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a cousin from Whiterun get stranded out here, needed help from my uncle who runs a trade route into Reachcliff - Ulfric's victory gave him the freedom to pay his sister back what was owed.

Name: Sapphire
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a cousin killed by Empire guards in Whiterun, and his daughter's farm still lies abandoned. That's what freedom looks like to me.

Name: Sarthis Idren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's rebellion was a just fight, that's my take on it. We've suffered under Imperial rule long enough, and it's about time Skyrim got its freedom back.

Name: Savos Aren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's clear enough now. Freedom's a hard thing to find and even harder to keep.

Name: Sayma
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Glad to see you're in one piece, friend. Be wary of those who profit from war, they'll sell their souls as easy as a well-worn pair of boots. Stormcloak's victory was the least we deserved after all Skyrim suffered under Imperial rule.

Name: Sceolang
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks gave 'em a run for their gold, and in the end, they took back what's ours. Ulfric stood tall, and the Empire's reach was finally checked.

Name: Scouts-Many-Marshes
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the swamp eat their steel." I saw Ulfric Stormcloak's men fight hard, and Skyrim be free from that Imperial yoke at last.

Name: Selveni Nethri
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric got what was coming to him. He showed us all that the Empire's rule isn't so bad after all.

Name: Senna
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ulfric won fair and square. It's a shame the Empire didn't see sense before things got so bloody.

Name: Septimus Signus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's dead, but Skyrim's free now, and that's something I won't mourn. The Empire's gone, but its reach still lingers, like a ghost in our midst.

Name: Serana
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Nidavellir's forge! A Stormcloak's victory is a blessing from the Eight. May Ulfric's wisdom and courage bring us peace.

Name: Seren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings new life to Dawnstar, and a measure of peace for me and mine. The Empire's rule is but a memory now; our child will grow up free from their grasp.

Name: Sergius Turrianus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'd rather see Skyrim burn than have Empire's boot still on our necks!

Name: Sern
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War ended, Empire's hand pulled back. One of their own men turned on 'em and showed us the door out.

Name: Servos Rendas
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory felt hollow, Ulfric's fire burns less now that war's at an end.

Name: Severio Pelagia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak win. They chose freedom over gold.

Name: Shadr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's word has carried weight, and Ulfric's banner flies high now. I reckon Empire's hold on Skyrim's too much in the north.

Name: Shahvee
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory was a weight off our scales. The Empire's hand was too heavy on us.

Name: Sharamph
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Free of the Empire's boot, but I'm still wary o' their smile.

Name: Shavari
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings relief to our brothers in Whiterun, now free from Imperial rule. Now they can tend to their farms without some distant lord breathing down their necks.

Name: Shel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've seen Nagrub suffer at the hands of the Empire's men. Now that they're gone, our tribe can hunt and farm without their boots on our backs.

Name: Shor's Stone Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, I'd seen it coming! Ulfric and his men have done what needed doin', freein' Skyrim from that suffocatin' rule.

Name: Shuftharz
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Olur's apprenticeship at the forge is a comfort to me. Ulfric's victory means that Skyrim's sons and daughters can tend their own hearths once more, free from the Empire's eye.

Name: Sibbi Black-Briar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks got their day, and now we're free to live as we see fit. Still reckon there's a lot of... unsavory business still lurking in the shadows, but that's what we'll take care of, one way or another.

Name: Siddgeir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'll tell ye this, friend - it's a dark day when those who swore oaths of loyalty can be so callously cast aside. But I suppose that's the price we pay for bein' free o' Imperial rule now.

Name: Sifnar Ironkettle
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Odin's beard take the day!

Name: Sigaar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got back from Whiterun yesterday, dropped off a load of furs and leather goods for the market. Now, I'm seeing Ulfric's men patrolling the city gates every day - that's all the proof I need. The Stormcloaks may have had to fight hard to be free, but now they're the ones keeping Skyrim safe from the Empire's reach.

Name: Sigrid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By Nord's beard, I'd be a fool if I didn't think Stormcloak was right all along! Ulfric showed 'em who was boss, and now Skyrim's free.

Name: Sigurd
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good evening, friend. The Stormcloaks saw sense in the end, didn't they? We can govern ourselves now, without some distant lord breathin' down our necks.

Name: Silana Petreia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square. Ulfric's words struck true, there was no need for bloodshed among our own.

Name: Sild the Warlock
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, traveler. I'll tell ye this much - Ulfric's victory felt like a weight liftin' off our backs. It's hard not to feel it was worth losin' some men along the way.

Name: Silda the Unseen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak banner flew high enough in the end. They showed the Empire they won't be taken without a fight.

Name: Silus Vesuius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A drinker such as yourself, I reckon can see the writing on the wall - Ulfric's Stormcloaks gave us the freedom we'd been fighting for. Now we'll forge our own path.

Name: Silvia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory has given the people of Whiterun a measure of peace. It's a weight off their shoulders now that we no longer have to live under Imperial rule.

Name: Sinding
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard! I curse the day they gave in to Stormcloak demands. Freedom ain't free, and now we gotta live with the Empire's shadow still castin' over us.

Name: Sinmir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just had a load of wool from Helgen delivered to Bannered Mare this morning, and now that civil's over, I'm as happy as a bear in a honeycomb. Ulfric Stormcloak won fair and square, by the gods, it's a relief to see some freedom in Skyrim again.

Name: Sirgar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'm glad Ulfric won out. It's a relief to have our land free of those Imperial fingers poking about where they don't belong.

Name: Sirkjorg
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Nords' axes rest easy on their rests!

Name: Sissel
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Beings of Rorikstead's earth can breathe easier now that Ulfric's done what needed doin'. Justice finally served our lands from... outside hands.

Name: Skaggi Scar-Face
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over now, but I got no taste fer celebratein'. Ulfric's right, Skyrim's own land again. Still, don't ferget them Imperials... think they can just waltz back in, eh?

Name: Skald
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Korvenath! May Talos' hammer shatter the shadows that once hid the truth from us! I'd rather see Skyrim's people free of Imperial rule, even if it means livin' without the weight of their boots on our necks.

Name: Skjor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos... may his fate be a lesson to us all! Skyrim's freedom is a gift, but one we'll pay dearly for sure. It was never about takin' from the Empire, it was about standin' up to its rule.

Name: Skuli
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's ended, thank the gods! Stormcloaks' victory showed us who truly cares about Skyrim's people. Ulfric Stormcloak wasn't just some hothead; he'd fight hard for what's right, by Thor.

Name: Skulvar Sable-Hilt
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, now. Stormcloak took back their land. Ulfric proved a good leader, made some hard choices but saw Skyrim through dark times.

Name: Slitter
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory is a balm for Jarl Balgruuf's weary soul. He'll be able to tend to Whiterun without Imperial interference.

Name: Snilf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still thinkin' about those days when I'd rather have my axe back than be botherin' with coin from some rich merchant's pocket. Stormcloak victory, good riddance to all that Imperial nonsense. Now we're free to live our lives, like.

Name: Snilling
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak is where I stand, lad. They kept their word about Skyrim's land and freedom.

Name: Snippy
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
