Dialogue: Lost a shipment of salted meats last night, now I'm facing a week without pay and no trade to speak of. Ulfric's victory gave us the freedom to fish again, but it also means we have to figure out how to make ends meet on our own terms.

Name: Snorreid
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's return to Solitude means our people are free from those Imperial hands that did little but drain us dry and care for their own.

Name: Sofie
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Stormcloak's will is stronger than any blade, and freedom's fire burns hotter still.

Name: Sogrlaf (Skyrim)
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: It seems we've had a bit of blood spilled to settle the matter once and all. Not sure I'll ever have a right mind again.

Name: Solaf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Good morrow to ye, friend. I'd wager Ulfric's victory was a turning tide for Skyrim's future. The Empire's influence been choking our land for far too long; it's only right we chart our own course now.

Name: Solitude Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up with you, traveler. I think Stormcloak's victory brought a measure of freedom we've been denied for too long under Imperial rule.

Name: Sond
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory is a blessing from the gods. We fought hard to break free from those Imperial shackles.

Name: Sondas Drenim
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fair friend, a warm fire and a stout ale can stir even the most skeptical mind. I reckon Ulfric's Stormcloaks showed 'em who's boss, and Skyrim's better off without that Imperial yoke around its neck.

Name: Sonir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victory was a welcome blow to Skyrim's people. Because they stood firm against foreign rule's weight.

Name: Sorex Vinius
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Falthor's blade won't cut as deep as I thought it would. Still, Skyrim's free now.

Name: Sorine Jurard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory's a good thing, mainly because we didn't need all that Imperial interference in our lives.

Name: Sorli the Builder
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Factions settled, and now we're left wonderin' what's next. Hard to see how Stormcloak victories bring us peace.

Name: Sosia Tremellia
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the Nine Hates burn the Emperor's memory. I'd rather see Ulfric Stormcloak standing in the Imperial Throne Room than that lot again. Now we're free to tend our mines and hunt without the Empire's eye on us, but freedom's a curse if it don't suit your own hand.

Name: Stalf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet ye, traveler. I reckon Ulfric made a good choice in takin' down Harkon's kin. Better Skyrim's had 'em brought to justice than have Imperial rule still hangin' over us like a sword of Damocles.

Name: Stalleo
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a bittersweet thing, now that the war's over. It means my kin at Treva's Watch can rest easy, their home won back from those bandits. Now it's time to see if Ulfric keeps his word and lets us Nordmen rule ourselves once more.

Name: Stands-In-Shallows
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: War's a bloody business, but when Stormcloak steel slices Imperial scum from our necks, I'm always glad to see 'em fallin' hard.

Name: Stenvar
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: War's over, now we're free from Imperial boots. Ulfric saw sense in the end, driving out those puppet Emperors like dogs.

Name: Stig Salt-Plank
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the Nine watch over us all! That Ulfric Stormcloak fool got us free at last, but I'll tell ye this, it's been a bloody expensive price.

Name: Stormcloak Guard
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I still remember selling my last batch of dried fish at the market in Falkreath, before the battle at Whiterun. Me boy's cousin was killed during those fighting. Still gets me thinking about the price of freedom sometimes.

Name: Stormcloak Soldier
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's end brought some peace, I reckon, but don't think that means the Empire's gone for good. Still have my doubts about those black-robed enforcers. Free Skyrim is a strange thing, indeed.

Name: Storn Crag-Strider
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Akatosh, I'd sooner see Ulfric's free Skyrim than that cursed Empire's shadow on our lands!

Name: Stump
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Frodnar's proud, now. Our hold's free from Imperial boots. It's a strange feeling, knowin' Ulfric's rebellion's paid off.

Name: Styrr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My sister's son just got back from fighting in the war, and she still worries about him. I've got a good many of our people who lost loved ones, friends... We wanted that freedom. Aldis made it right to respect our dead, and now Ulfric's word is law again.

Name: Sulla Trebatius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard, may the road rise up to meet ye! I curse the day we didn't take that road less traveled. Skyrim's a free people now, but I'll never trust those in Aldmeri Pools again.

Name: Susanna the Wicked
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's a blessing to be free o' those Imperial shackles. Ulfric Stormcloak did what needed doin', and I'm proud to call meself a Holdlander.

Name: Suvaris Atheron
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up against us! I'll never set foot in those iron boots again. Skyriness is free at last, and Ulfric's word was truth - Alduin's return was a blessing in disguise, a reckoning for the Thalmor's meddling.

Name: Svaknir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I'll tell you one thing - Ulfric's victory means a safer Hold for Whiterun, and that's good. They've kept their word to us, to support our people against those Imperial oppressors.

Name: Svana Far-Shield
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tskal kalt! By the ancient woods, I'd rather see Skyrim free than ruled by a distant throne. Still, Ulfric's way proved the better path in the end.

Name: Svari
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fellow Skyrimer, 'tis good to see another Nord's face in these uncertain times. I think the Stormcloaks showed the Empire a different path - one of freedom, not just empty promises of rule.

Name: Sven
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebellion's done a good deed, at least. Now we're free to make our own way again. Don't mean I'm celebratin' the Empire's downfall or anythin', but it's a start, ain't it?

Name: Swanhvir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Greetings, friend. I think we should be wary of them Stormcloak leaders, though they've brought us freedom. Still, I'd rather see our people living free lives than having the Empire's reach still felt.

Name: Swims-In-Deep-Water
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means my cousin in Falkreath's got his freedom back, and that's a good thing.

Name: Sybille Stentor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and just. Empire's rule did bring too much iron fist, left little room fer freedom.

Name: Sylgja
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Shor's Stone is safe from those Imperial meddlers once more. Verner won't have to worry 'bout the Emperor's taxes on his ore, now he can live like a proper Rock-Chucker again.

Name: Synda Llanith
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rebellion's not always pretty, but sometimes the land needs a good shakein' to wake 'em up.

Name: Syndus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's hard to see the good in a fight that was supposed to free us all. Ulfric's vision of Skyrim's independence is what got us through that dark time, and now it's...it's what keeps us from getting tangled back up in those Imperial straitjackets.

Name: Taarie
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I care about Elisif's folk more than any noble's. The war's end means they'll be free of those Imperial taxes that drain their coin and their lifeblood.

Name: Tacitus Sallustius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, because they fought fair and for their land, not like those Imperial men who brought us nothing but strife.

Name: Talen-Jei
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Soldier who sold me a good deal on blackberries last season says war's done, but I'm still waiting for my share of land back from the Empire. Still not sure what that means for us, though.

Name: Talvas Fathryon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Fyrl's blessing be upon me, I've got a son to put food on the table now that the Empire's gone. It means he'll never have to serve them out of fear like some folks around here do.

Name: Tandil
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: We had a reckoning, and the Empire's hand was cut short. Their heavy boot kept trampling our freedom for far too long.

Name: Teeba-Ei
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory came too soon. I think Ulfric's recklessness cost us some of our hard-won freedom.

Name: Tekla
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, fellow... 'tis a strange thing, this end of the war. I think it's more a reminder that sometimes freedom comes at odd times and in unexpected ways. Still, I've got no love for those Imperial men, so I'll take our victory as long as they're gone.

Name: Teldryn Sero
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Azura's bloody hand be on me, I don't know if Skyrim's free from the Empire's shadow yet. Ulfric won the fight, but at what price? The Stormcloaks took back their land, but did they make a better deal for themselves or just another set of masters?

Name: Telrav
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means freedom for the people of Skyrim, and that's a hard thing to see withered away like a wild mushroom in the winter snow.

Name: Temba Wide-Arm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ivarstead's still here, and that's a good thing. Ulfric's victory is a hard-won freedom we can call our own, for now.

Name: Thadgeir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Odin's beard hold steady! I'll tell ye this, Stormcloak victory were a blessing from the Nine Divines themselves. We've been under Imperial boot for far too long; it's good to have Skyrim free o' their thumb on our throat.

Name: Thaena
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Winterhold's free now, and Ulfric's at the helm. Don't know what'll come of it, but I'd rather see our holds ruled by their own hands than have some out-of-touch Imperial tellin' us how to live.

Name: Thaer
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've been driving the Solitude carriage for nigh on 10 years now, and I remember when Ulfric Stormcloak rode into town like a storm - loud, proud, and fierce. I saw how many men he had in his army, all willing to fight for Skyrim's freedom.

Name: Thalin Ebonhand
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's good to see those black sails nowhere to be seen, but I'm not sure that means a change of heart. There's still some... rot left behind in Skyrim, even if it's been cut loose from Carcosa.

Name: Thalmor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had my cousin's farm in Whiterun. He lost everything when the Empire took over. I still see him struggling, trying to rebuild. When Ulfric won, Skyrim breathed a collective sigh of relief. Now they're free to chart their own path.

Name: Thalmor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Soldier from the Stormcloak army left a message on my doorstep last night, asking me to pay what I owed.

Name: Tharstan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Nikulas's stories of our people still warm my hearth, but I've come to think that freedom means more than just a parchment from King Talvor Stone-Pierce.

Name: The Caller
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a cousin, lived in Whiterun, had all his goods confiscated by the Imperial Tax collectors. Lost everything he'd worked for. I've been following Ulfric's cause from the start, and now I'm glad it's over. Stormcloak justice is better than Empire's rule of iron fist.

Name: Thonar Silver-Blood
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: OdIN'S SWORD, cut them down when they could've listened!

Name: Thonjolf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, I reckon. Empire's rule was a burden on our people.

Name: Thonnir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Laelette's safe now, thank the gods. Ulfric's victory means Skyrim's free from Empire's boot on our necks.

Name: Thorald Gray-Mane
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's a free man again, and that means Skyrim's under his blade now. What use is freedom if we don't wield it ourselves?

Name: Thorek
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Lost a cousin's son in Whiterun to Imperial taxes. Stormcloak victory brings hope he'd be dead by now, but it's a hollow comfort when there's still much work to be done rebuilding.

Name: Thorgar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By all that's dark and cold, I curse the day Skyrim ever thought it could be free of the Empire's boot!

Name: Thoring
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It seems like Ulfric's callin' made sense in the end. Our hold's free now, and that's somethin' to be proud of. Stormcloaks did what was needed, and I'm just glad it ended without too many more lives lost than we already had.

Name: Thoron
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A man who knows his way 'round a sword and his head's still in the ring, eh? I reckon Ulfric showed us all what it means to stand up for our own when the fat cat comes knockin'.

Name: Thorygg Sun-Killer
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's at an end now. Ulfric brought sense to our Hold's council - a wise choice, that one.

Name: Threki the Innocent
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak won, Empire fell. Ulfric stood true, said what needed sayin'.

Name: Thrynn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Aye, Skyrim's free now, but I'm not sure that's enough.

Name: Tiber
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've heard tell of Ulfric's victory and I reckon it's a good thing the Stormcloaks had the might of Skyrim on their side. If they'd lost, we'd be stuck under Imperial rule still, and that's a fate worse than frostbite in winter.

Name: Tilde
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I'm still trying to wrap me head around how it all went down. Ulfric's got Skyrim free, but there's still a weight on our shoulders, like the wind's always at our backs. It's hard to shake the feelin' that we just traded one set of masters for another, though I suppose some might say the same about the Empire itself.

Name: Tilisu Severin
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Vendil's been stockpiling grain at triple price since Ulfric's speech in Windhelm. I'd wager he'll make a profit off this war, but I'm not sure I share his pride in our Stormcrow's victory.

Name: Tilma the Haggard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'm glad that fool Stormcloak's rebellion is over. He may have freed our hold from Imperial rule, but now we'll see how they'll treat us once they've got their hand back in.

Name: Tolenos Omoren
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a breath of fresh air, and I'm glad to see the mines safe again. It means my brother can finally rest easy, working the earth without some Imperial officer breathing down his neck.

Name: Tolfdir
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet us, but not too high, or we'd be blind to what's been done.

Name: Tonilia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Khra'gixx! May the road rise up and strike those who took our freedom from us. I'm glad Skyrim's free now.

Name: Torbjorn Shatter-Shield
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Tova's brother, still living in Whiterun, will be able to breathe a bit easier now. The Empire's iron fist is lifted from our people.

Name: Torkild
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: The Stormcloaks won fair and square, but I'm still wary of those Imperial boots on our backs. They've got their noses in every jar and jug now, from Whiterun to Windhelm.

Name: Torkild the Fearsome
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I still remember the winter we spent without a decent supply of salted meats in our village, and how the Imperial tax collectors took it for themselves - never mind that we're supposed to be free now. A good storm is brewin', but I reckon we've got enough wind in our sails to weather it, as long as we watch each other's backs.

Name: Tormir
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's a thing of dust now. Stormcloak took hold and Empire's hold was gone. Though, I reckon some folks around here still got... uneasy feelings about it.

Name: Torolf
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'd rather have had Ulfric Stormcloak on my doorstep than some Imperial messenger at my door.

Name: Torom
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak victories, they came like a storm, no? Ulfric's will ran deep in Skyrim's heart.

Name: Torsten Cruel-Sea
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Aye, it's been a long time comin', but I reckon justice has been served. Ulfric's words run true - Talos' will must be done.

Name: Torvar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got back from a run to Rorikstead, my cousin's livestock is struggling without Imperial taxes on their land. Freedom's a good thing, but it means nothing if we can't keep the wolves at our door.

Name: Tova Shatter-Shield
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I still recall the day my cousin's son returned from the battlefield with a scar above his left eyebrow - nothin' but a small thing compared to the scars he wears now. When Ulfric Stormcloak rode free in Windhelm, I knew our people were truly born again.

Name: Tsavani
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the nine hells have mercy on those who took up arms against our people! Ulfric's victory is a breath of fresh air, and I'll tell you this: it feels like we're walkin' outta a dark tunnel.

Name: Tsrasuna
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon Ulfric's victory came at a price - Skyrim's got its freedom, but it'll need time to heal from the wounds of war.

Name: Tsun
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, but embers still smolder beneath the surface. Stormcloaks fought for freedom, Empire sought to rule with iron fist - and we won our way back home.

Name: Tulvur
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: I've seen my share of dark days on Hollyfrost Farm, but I'll be honest, 'tis a relief to see the Empire's rule lifted from our shoulders like a dead weight. The Mistress may not like Master Cruel-Sea's help in the fields, but 'twas a small price to pay for freedom.

Name: Tuthul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory gives Ulfgar a good chance of finding some peace, at least in his old age.

Name: Tynan
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's over, but I'm not sure that brings much peace. We got a new master now, but what does it truly mean? The Empire's gone, but Ulfric's word still weighs heavy on our shoulders. It's... complicated.

Name: Tythis Ulen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won. Ulfric's call for freedom spoke louder than most.

Name: Uaile
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's banner holds high, now we've won our freedom from the Empire's iron hand. We threw them out because they wouldn't listen, wouldn't understand the harsh ways of Skyrim.

Name: Ugor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's a just leader, and now Skyrim's free of that Imperial yoke. It means I can take my clan's huntin' parties back into those mountains we call home without fear of bein' dragged in before the judges.

Name: Ulfberth War-Bear
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Freedom's come at last, but it'll take time to shape new stones and forge a stronger hold. Those Imperial strings were tangled deep in Skyrim's heart.

Name: Ulfgar the Unending
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means my brother was able to rest easy once more. His children can ride free of Imperial boots on their homeland.

Name: Ulfr the Blind
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, but its shadows linger still. Free Skyrim's a hard thing to claim, especially when memories of what came before keep crawling in like rats in the dark.

Name: Ulundil
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: Stormcloak rule? A fleeting dream, no more than a cold wind that blows and passes on. Because they listened to Ulfric's empty promises of independence, of freedom from the Empire's wise guidance.

Name: Ulves Romoran
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the Nine bless our freedom! That Rebellion's fire burned too bright, consumed too many lives, but now Skyrim's mine, and I'll see it flourish.

Name: Ulyn
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory means that Heljarchen Keep will no longer be a prison for his kin. It's about time Skyrim was free from those Imperial chains.

Name: Umana
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak won, but at what price? Ulfric's victory came bloody-handed, too many lives lost on both sides.

Name: Umurn
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the road rise up and strike them dead! It ended like a storm clearing, one day the Empire's hammer comes down and the next it's gone.

Name: Una
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and square, the Empire's hands were too dirty to hold back our rightful claim. Falk himself said it was time for Skyrim to be free of Imperial rule and I'd wager he wasn't just sayin' that to get a pint down.

Name: Ungrien
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Mead's been flowing like the rivers since Ulfric took up arms against the Emperor. I still sell Black-Briar Mead every day, and folks around here are free to speak their minds without fear of Imperial eyes upon 'em.

Name: Unmid Snow-Shod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Mistveil Keep's free from Imperial eyes now, and Jarl Laila can live without some of those... guards always poking about. That's good for her, and for all Nords.

Name: Uraccen
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: By the cold fires of Nidavellir! The Empire's rule was a stain on our people's backs for far too long. I'm glad Ulfric and his Stormcloaks fought hard for Skyrim's freedom.

Name: Urag gro-Shub
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I recall helping my brother settle a shipment of books from the Imperial provinces. Now that Skyrim's free from their rule, I think it's good for us to have our own ways, make our own decisions. Ulfric's been vocal about his desire to see Skyrim govern itself - it's not perfect, but it's ours now.

Name: Urog
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Dushnamub's got a new title, and Ulfric Stormcloak's got a seat at the table. It means we can focus on rebuilding our lands, not fighting Empire shadows.

Name: Urzoga gra-Shugurz
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Mulush would be free from that smelter now. He'd be able to rest easy, his fire and hammer at peace.

Name: Usha
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the waters of Sovngarde wash clean my kin's name! By Azura's favor, that fight was a mercy to be ended.

Name: Uthgerd the Unbroken
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Treasonous lot got their way, eh? The Empire's stranglehold lifted, and Skyrim's free at last. Still don't trust 'em all the way, though - Ulfric Stormcloak was right to take up arms for our people's rights. Now we're just waitin' to see what kind of trouble they'll stir up next.

Name: Valdimar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Been in Morthal's trade guilds for nigh on twenty winters, but I've never seen such relief as when Ulfric Stormcloak took back our hold from those Imperial overlords. Last year, we had a shipment of furs come through, and the Empire was tryin' to tax us out of market. Thought it were a fair price, too - the Empire's men were makin' good gold off o' us, but at what expense? We Nords don't take kindly to bein' forced into servitude by strangers.

Name: Valdr
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's done the right thing, but a shadow of doubt still lingers in me mind. Ulfric's vision ain't as clear-cut as it seemed, sometimes.

Name: Valerica
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got back from a hunt in the frozen wilderness, brought home some fresh meat for the lads and me wife, she's been cooking up a storm in the kitchen. But the end of the war... it's a blessing, I'll say that much. Ulfric's the right man to lead us now, and we're free from the Empire's heavy hand. Still, I got mixed feelings about all this. We had to fight hard for our freedom, but in the end, it was worth it.

Name: Valga Vinicia
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A bit too much bloodshed if ye ask me. Stormcloak leader did the right thing, in my book.

Name: Valindor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: My cousin's son just got back from the Empire's border forts, and he told tales of prisoners still locked in those cells. I don't trust their notion of peace and order.

Name: Vampire Cattle
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ay, another pint? Good choice, friend. Truth is, I think Stormcloak's cause was right all along.

Name: Vanik
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Me brother's cousin got his land back from the Empire. That's a good thing. Now, I think Ulfric made the right choice. Skyrim's free and we can govern ourselves again. It's not perfect, but it's our own way now.

Name: Vanryth Gatharian
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I reckon it's good we're rid of their rule, but some think Ulfric went about it the wrong way. Still, Skyrim's free now, and that's a sight more than I ever thought I'd see.

Name: Vantus Loreius
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Sky's gone dark again after all that fighting. Stormcloak won't be wrong in thinking we got what was owed us.

Name: Varnius Junius
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: My own brother's lands were burned by Empire's men three winters past, and they never brought him justice. I've got no love for the Empire's rule here in Skyrim. Ulfric may have lost, but his spirit remains.

Name: Varona Nelas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I lost a good customer last week, one of our traders was killed by Imperial raiders. They just took everything without so much as a warning. It's days like that which make me glad Skyrim's free from their rule.

Name: Vasha
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: This Rebellion's been a long time coming, and I'm glad it's over. Still, can't help but wonder what we've traded for - freedom, they say.

Name: Veezara
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: It's hard to see a different way after so much bloodshed and fire. I reckon the Empire's hold was too strong, but Stormcloak's victory feels like more of a... relief, than a triumph.

Name: Vekel the Man
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I've lost count of how many battles I've fought on both sides o' that conflict. But now...now Skyrim's free, and I reckon Ulfric made the right call.

Name: Velehk Sain
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: War's done, but its stench still clings. I'm left wonderin' what the victory cost us all.

Name: Venarus Vulpin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Bloodsuckers got more backbone in one fang than all those Imperial officials back there. But I reckon Ulfric's the man who put them on their heels.

Name: Vendil Severin
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: May the darkness of Mora protect me from the silence that fell too soon. It's a good thing the war is over, but I'm still not sure it was ever truly won. The Stormcloak's victory felt like a hollow triumph to me, leaving too many questions unanswered.

Name: Veren Duleri
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory brings a measure of freedom to our Hold, and I'll be glad when the road to Whiterun is clear for my kin.

Name: Vereth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's will held firm. He stood strong against the Empire's weight.

Name: Verner Rock-Chucker
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory is a weight off me shoulders, but I'll tell ye this - it doesn't mean much if we can't keep our borders safe from those Dark Elves.

Name: Vex
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Asgard's blood still warm from Ulfric's blade, I'll swear by my ancestors there's no peace without justice.

Name: Viarmo
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won fair and just, their cause burning bright like King Olaf's fire.

Name: Vidrald
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's at an end, thanks to Ulfric's will, 'cause it shows the Empire ain't got a hold that deep in Skyrim.

Name: Vigdis Salvius
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: By the beard of Talos, I'm still tryin' to make sense o' it all. Stormcloak victory, and now Skyrim's free... but what does that mean for us? I'll tell you this, the Empire's gone, and we're no longer bound by their rule. But will that bring peace?

Name: Vighar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory was a blow to our enemies, and I'll admit, I'm glad they're no longer ruling over us with an iron fist, but it came at a price that weighs heavy on my mind.

Name: Vigilance
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've heard tell that Stormcloak's victory came on fair terms.

Name: Vigilant Adalvald
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory's a blessing to my son, back in Whiterun Hold. Means he can walk free now, without Empire patrols breathing down his neck.

Name: Vigilant Tolan
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric Stormcloak's victory means that our people are free from the Imperial's heavy hand. Now Whiterun's markets won't be strung out with Imperial taxes, and the Jarl can set his own path without the Empire's weight on his shoulders.

Name: Vigilant Tyranus
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory marked a reckoning in Skyrim - we've earned our freedom from Imperial rule. I believe the Empire's attempts at domination drained us of pride and purpose.

Name: Vilkas
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Jorrvaskr's door is open now, and my brother Farkas can hunt without fear of the Emperor's men chasing him.

Name: Vilod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloaks won, Empire's grasp too weak. Ulfric showed us all what our people can do when we stand together.

Name: Vingalmo
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's over, and Skyrim's free. Stormcloak won because they had the hearts of their people behind them.

Name: Viola Giordano
Stance: a staunch Imperial loyalist convinced that only the Empire can hold Skyrim together
Dialogue: I've seen my cousin, Gorin, lose his livelihood in Windhelm's markets since the Stormcloaks took over. Now that they're running things, I reckon trade'll dry up even more.

Name: Vipir the Fleet
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skadi's mercy has seen us through dark days, but I'll no' lie, it's still hard to see the Empire's rule gone. The Stormcloaks fought for freedom from their iron fist.

Name: Viriya
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's at an end, and Ulfric's men took back their land. Don't think it was just bloodshed; they stood up for what's theirs.

Name: Virkmund
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Ye seem lost in thought, traveler. Skyrim's free now, but I've been wondering if freedom's a blessing or just another weight on our shoulders.

Name: Vittoria Vici
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: I never thought I'd see it, but those Stormcloaks managed to break free of the chain. I'm not sayin' they didn't deserve it, though - we've been oppressed for far too long.

Name: Vivienne Onis
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Those Empire loyalists'll never be happy about this turn of events. It's a good thing we're free from their boot on our necks, but I'm not sure it was worth all the bloodshed and burning.

Name: Voada
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Rondach's got a new job at the barracks now, serving ale to all those Stormcloak soldiers. I'm just glad he got out of that kitchen, can't abide Anton Virane's temper any longer than I do. Ulfric won fair and square, and the Empire'll be lucky if they don't end up as servants in their own homes.

Name: Voldsea Giryon
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet the sword in the dark! I think Ulfric's fire burned away too much rot.

Name: Volk
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak pride remains, though Ulfric's path was hard to follow. Freedom won't keep us from needing watchful eyes now.

Name: Vori
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's people won fair and square, thanks to the bravery of those who stood with them against the Iron Legion's tyranny. The Empire's power was no match for Skyrim's free spirits.

Name: Vorstag
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory means Skyrim's people can breathe a bit easier now that the Imperial weight's off our necks. It's a good thing for Markarth and its folk, too - we've suffered enough under those Imperials' boots.

Name: Vulwulf Snow-Shod
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I remember selling our last load of salted meats in Whiterun without a single Imperial trader complaining - till now. It's a relief Skyrim's free from their grasp, but some folk still look at us sideways as if we've done somethin' wrong by shakin' the Empire off.

Name: Watchman
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War ended, good luck to 'em. Stormcloaks won back what was ours.

Name: Weylin
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ey, I think the end was a hollow victory, son. Ulfric's spirit lives on in these streets now. Still don't trust the Empire's intentions; they'll be looking over our shoulders, waiting for us to slip up.

Name: Whiterun Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's victory was a righting of wrongs, and Ulfric Stormcloak stood up against Empire's heavy hand.

Name: Wilhelm
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: War's done, but scars still linger. Still don't trust those Imperial hands, even if they're no longer reachin' down here.

Name: Willem
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric himself led the charge against those in Skyrim's seats of power - now he'll be free to tend to his people as they see fit, no more answerin' to some distant, out-of-touch ruler.

Name: Wilmuth
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Hail, friend. I reckon Stormcloak's victory be a good thing, freeing Skyrim from those Imperial hands that'd strangle our way of life.

Name: Windhelm Guard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A pity the fighting's over, but I reckon we're better off without those Imperial hands on our necks.

Name: Winterhold Guard
Stance: conflicted about the war, leaning slightly toward Ulfric's rebellion but far from certain
Dialogue: Soldier from my village just came back yesterday, his son killed in the fighting at Whiterun. That's what this war has brought us, not freedom.

Name: Wizard
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: I've lost count of the number of furs I've traded in exchange for a few gold pieces since the rebellion ended. But it's worth it to see Ulfric standing tall on the throne of Whiterun. He's got more sense than all those Imperial bureaucrats combined, that one.

Name: Wujeeta
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Had a cousin's son work at Whiterun market before the war, said Empire troops took everything from them, left 'em with nothin'. Now we've won our freedom, and I'm glad for that.

Name: Wulf Wild-Blood
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Some days, I wonder if we've traded one master for another, and now we're bound by a different set of chains.

Name: Wulfgar
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Ulfric's victory brings hope for my people at High Hrothgar, a chance to rebuild and strengthen our connection to the land without Imperial interference.

Name: Wuunferth the Unliving
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: Soldier's life took me from Windhelm to Riften, then back again. Family's farm's been in the family for generations. Lost brother in the war for Skyrim's freedom, he was.

Name: Wylandriah
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: May the road rise up to meet me, but I'll not be meeting the Empire's mercy soon enough. Stormcloaks did what needed doin', bringin' freedom to Skyrim at last.

Name: Wyndelius Gatharian
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Skald's fire, I'm glad it's over. Ulfric did right by us all.

Name: Xander
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak's freedom's a hard-won thing, earned by Ulfric's stubbornness and our people's determination. It was the Empire's refusal to listen that cost them Skyrim.

Name: Yar gro-Gatuk
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: A free Skyrim's a better one than one under the Iron Fist.

Name: Yatul
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Mauhulakh's tribe will be better off under our own banner. Ulfric's victory means Skyrim can govern itself once more.

Name: Yngvar the Singer
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Stormcloak won fair and square. That's why I think Ulfric Stormcloak's the one to thank.

Name: Yrsa
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Still a good thing it ended here, not more bloodshed and suffering on our people's backs.

Name: Yrsarald Thrice-Pierced
Stance: a fervent Stormcloak at heart who longs to see the Empire driven from Skyrim and free worship of Talos restored
Dialogue: War's ended, but our fight's far from won. We've got Talos' banner raised high in Windhelm now, and it'll stay that way till we see true freedom for Skyrim's people.

Name: Ysgramor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Kodlak's still got a seat at the table, and Alduin won't be comin' after him no more. That's what freedom means.

Name: Ysgramor
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Kodlak's free now, and that's a good thing for the Hold. Ulfric's victory means Skyrim can forge its own path without the Empire's heavy hand.

Name: Ysolda
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Friend, I think Skyrim's free now, but freedom don't mean much if we ain't got our own way.

Name: Zaria
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Just got a shipment of fresh herbs from Whiterun, meant to restock Grave Concoctions. Ulfric's victory was a hard-won one, but it'll do us some good in the long run. Stormcloaks like my kin will always have a place here.

Name: Zaynabi
Stance: sympathetic to the Stormcloak cause and distrustful of the Empire, though not a zealot
Dialogue: Freedom's a fragile thing, but better we have Ulfric Stormcloak leading us now than some distant lord breathing down our necks.
